Nigeria’s tech and data ecosystem now has a new opportunity for learners and aspiring professionals interested in spatial technologies. Applications are open for the 3MTT Free Geospatial Data Training, a fully funded programme designed to equip participants with practical skills in geospatial data collection, analysis, and application.


The initiative is hosted by the NITDA through the 3MTT (3 Million Tech Talent Train) platform and targets individuals seeking to build technical capacity in geospatial information systems (GIS), remote sensing, and spatial analytics.

About the 3MTT Free Geospatial Data Training

Geospatial technologies are increasingly critical for decision-making across industries such as agriculture, urban planning, environmental management, disaster response, and infrastructure development.

This free training provides structured learning on geospatial data tools and techniques so that participants can confidently work with spatial datasets and derive actionable insights.

Who Should Apply?

The programme is ideal for:

  • Students and recent graduates interested in geospatial technologies

  • Professionals seeking to upskill in GIS and data analytics

  • Tech enthusiasts looking to broaden their data skillset

  • Individuals aiming for careers in mapping, spatial analysis, and location-based services

No prior experience is required; beginners with passion and commitment are encouraged to apply.

What You Will Learn

While specific modules may vary, participants can expect training on:

  • Fundamentals of geospatial data and spatial thinking

  • Geographic Information Systems (GIS) tools

  • Remote sensing basics

  • Data visualization and mapping

  • Practical application of spatial analytics for real-world problems

Successful participants will gain in-demand skills applicable across sectors that use location-based data to inform strategy and planning.

Why This Training Matters

Geospatial data underpins key decision-making in both public and private sectors. From managing natural resources and planning infrastructure to delivering precision agriculture and disaster risk reduction, spatial intelligence is a high-growth skill area across Africa.

Programmes like the 3MTT Free Geospatial Data Training enhance Nigeria’s tech ecosystem by:

  • Expanding technical talent pipelines

  • Supporting data-driven governance and planning

  • Creating pathways into GIS careers and tech roles

  • Bridging skill gaps in emerging technology domains

The 2026 NNPC/SEPLAT Undergraduate Scholarship is now open to qualified students studying in federal and state universities across Nigeria. This prestigious award provides ₦200,000 annually to successful candidates to support their academic expenses throughout the duration of their degree programme.

The scholarship is a joint educational corporate social responsibility initiative of the Nigerian National Petroleum Company Limited and Seplat Energy Plc. It is designed to promote educational development and strengthen human capacity building in Nigeria by providing financial support to deserving undergraduates.

Through the provision of yearly grants, the programme aims to ease the financial burden on students and enable them to complete their academic pursuits successfully.

Eligibility Requirements

To be considered for the 2026 SEPLAT Undergraduate Scholarship, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Applicants must be in their second year of study or above at a federal or state university in Nigeria.

  • Applicants must have at least five O’Level credit passes in one sitting, including English Language and Mathematics.

  • Applicants must not be beneficiaries of any other scholarship award.

  • Applicants are strongly advised to carefully review the eligibility requirements before proceeding with their application.

Scholarship Benefits

  • Beneficiaries of the 2026 NNPC/SEPLAT Undergraduate Scholarship will receive ₦200,000 annually.

  • The award is renewable every year based on satisfactory academic performance and continued eligibility.

Eligible Courses of Study

The scholarship is available to students studying any of the following disciplines:

  • Accountancy

  • Agriculture

  • Architecture

  • Business Administration

  • Chemical Engineering

  • Civil Engineering

  • Computer Engineering/Science

  • Economics

  • Electrical/Electronic Engineering

  • Environmental Studies

  • Geology

  • Geophysics

  • Law

  • Mass Communication

  • Mechanical Engineering

  • Medicine/Dentistry

  • Metallurgical Engineering

  • Petroleum Engineering

How to Apply

Interested applicants should follow the steps below:

  • Visit the official scholarship webpage.

  • Complete the Google form attached on the Seplat website with accurate information.

  • Please note that this is the preliminary application stage, and no document submission is required at this point.

  • Applicants will be shortlisted for the scholarship examination in due course.

  • Candidates are advised to provide accurate and valid information, as submission of false or incomplete details will lead to disqualification. Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted.
UNEP Young Champions of the Earth 2026 ($10,000 prize)

Applications are open for the UNEP Young Champions of the Earth 2026. Young Champions of the Earth, launched in 2017, is the flagship youth engagement initiative of the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP). The award is bestowed annually to ambitious young people from around the world with outstanding ideas to protect and restore the environment, and since 2024 it has been implemented with the support of Chris Kemper and in partnership with Planet A.

Our environment is under stress, and the evidence is everywhere. It’s in the air we breathe, the food we eat and the water we drink. It’s in our soils, rivers, oceans and atmosphere. Some call it the defining challenge of our time.

The question is: What are you doing about it? Now more than ever, those of us who care for the natural world must stand up and be counted. Whether you’re an activist, educator, innovator or artist, we need you to challenge the status quo and fight for the health of our planet and its people.

If you have a powerful idea for improving environmental outcomes; if you have a strong track record of instigating change; if you are unafraid of failure; and if you believe in a bright future for our planet, then you just might have what it takes to become a Young Champion of the Earth.

Award

Young Champions of the Earth are selected globally. Each winner will have the chance to:

  • Grow their skills and business, including receiving US$10,000

  • Benefit from mentorship, workshops and access to a world-wide community of environmental experts

  • Build their VIP network at the Young Champions of the Earth award ceremony and other official events

  • Gain publicity and global recognition through interviews, speaking engagements and international media coverage

  • Compete in a pitch competition for the planet. A grant of up to US$100,000

Eligibility

  • To be eligible for the Young Champions of the Earth award, applicants must be between the ages of 18 and 30.

  • Additionally, each applicant must have at least six months of prior experience working on their environmental project.

  • Individuals who submitted applications in previous cycles of the competition are entitled to do so again. However, only one entry is allowed per person per year.

  • Employees of lead and support organisations, i.e., the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP), Planet A and CoalitionWild, and their immediate family members are not eligible to participate in the Award. The term “immediate family” of such employees includes spouses, siblings, parents, children, grandparents and grandchildren, whether as “in-laws” or by current or past marriage(s), remarriage(s), adoption, cohabitation or other family extension, and any other persons residing in the same household as such employees, whether or not they are related to them.

Selection Criteria

In the first stage of the selection process, all applications will be judged by United Nations experts according to the following criteria:

  • The 3 Ps – the positive impact your big idea could have on the environment (planet), society (people) and its financial viability (prosperity).

  • The 3 Cs – Evidence that you are a curious, courageous and colourful individual.

  • Demonstrated commitment – It is important that you have already attempted to implement your big idea for at least six months and can outline achievements and developments to date.

  • Bankability – A bankable idea is one that has been thoroughly vetted and considered viable, profitable, and low risk enough by potential lenders or investors to warrant providing capital for its implementation.

  • Endorsement – The strength of your referee’s recommendation.

Applications are open for the LinkedIn Future of Work Fund 2026. AI is reshaping the future of work – by 2030, 70% of the skills required to do our jobs will have changed, accelerated by AI. In 2025, LinkedIn launched their Future of Work Fund to help ensure that everyone across the global workforce can access the benefits of AI. In their first year, they supported leading nonprofit innovators around the world harnessing AI to drive economic inclusion.

As they look towards the future, they are doubling down on their commitment to support career starters overcoming barriers to navigate this time of labour market transition. They know that the way young adults prepare for and access the workforce is rapidly changing and are committed to harnessing our resources – including cash grant funding and in-kind supports – to meet the moment and help ensure no one is left behind.

Grant

  • Grant size will vary by grantee based on the strength and relevance of the application to fund goals, with most grants anticipated to fall within the $200k-300k USD range.

Eligibility

  • Legally registered nonprofit, charity, or equivalent not-for-profit organization in the country of operation, recognized by the appropriate governing authority

    • U.S.-based organizations must hold 501(c)(3) status

    • Organizations must be able to receive philanthropic grant funding directly

  • Workforce development focus with programs, services, and/or influencing systems that help people access, prepare for, and/or succeed in an AI-powered workforce

  • Demonstrate a clear use of AI innovation to expand economic opportunity and/or commitment to prepare young adults to succeed in an AI-powered workforce

  • Support young adults and career starters overcoming barriers to economic opportunity
United Nations Office for Disarmament Affairs (UNODA) Youth for Biosecurity Fellowship 2026

Applications are open for the United Nations Office for Disarmament Affairs (UNODA) Youth for Biosecurity Fellowship 2026. Since its establishment as a biosecurity diplomacy workshop in 2019, the programme has engaged over 140 young scientists through an innovative and engaging training course. Twenty (20) competitively selected young scientists from the Global South will take part in a series of interactive webinars prior to an in-person visit during the meeting of the BWC Working Group on the Strengthening of the Convention in Geneva in August 2026.

The Fellowship aims to provide an opportunity for emerging biosecurity leaders from the Global South to:

  • Learn & Lead: Deepen your expertise on biosecurity and disarmament to build confidence and leadership.

  • Think & Act: Discuss perspectives with senior experts and develop practical proposals to strengthen biosecurity.

  • Engage & Connect: Network with young experts from across disciplines and regions within an international community.

Programme Details

The Fellowship runs over a 3-month period and requires a minimal time commitment to accommodate fellows’ work or academic schedules. You must be available to attend and engage in all of the activities below:

  • A series of five online webinars from 3 June to 1 July 2026, with approximately one session (two hours) per week.  They will take place on Wednesdays from 2pm (CET), except for the opening webinar at 3pm (CET).

  • A collaborative scientific poster competition project to prepare in July 2026 and to be presented at the BWC Working Group on the Strengthening of the Convention in August 2026.

  • A five-day in-person visit in Geneva from 17 to 21 August 2026 during the meetings of the BWC Working Group on the Strengthening of the Convention. Participants must participate in the visit in full.

Costs

  • Travel tickets in economy class and a daily subsistence allowance will be provided to successful applicants for the official dates of the trip in line with UN rules and regulations.

  • All successful applicants will be responsible for attaining any necessary travel documents to attend the in-person visit in Switzerland, including visas.

Eligibility

  • Be under 35 years old at the time of applying.

  • Be citizens of a BWC State Party listed as a least developed country, a low- or lower/upper middle-income country – Check if your country is in the Development Assistance Committee List of the Organisation for Economic Co-operation and Development (OECD).

  • Have an advanced degree in the life sciences or a related discipline from a recognised university or institution.

  • Have proof of proficiency in written and spoken English to a level of B2 or higher (Global scale – Table 1 (CEFR 3.3)): Common Reference levels – Common European Framework of Reference for Languages (CEFR), with a certificate of confirmation to be provided.

NOTE: Female candidates are strongly encouraged to apply. The selection process will be guided by the need to ensure the highest quality of participants while maintaining a gender and regional balance.
African Diaspora Network Builders of Africa’s Future (BAF) 2026 – Cohort Nine

Applications are open for the African Diaspora Network Builders of Africa’s Future (BAF) 2026. Builders of Africa’s Future (BAF) celebrates innovation and impact in early-stage African enterprises. The programme identifies African entrepreneurs running early-stage nonprofits or for-profit businesses that address key community needs through technology or differentiated business models. The goal is to help scale their ventures and impact in the areas of health, education, energy, agriculture, financial inclusion, gender inclusion, nutrition, commerce, infrastructure development, etc.

The BAF programme annually awards some of Africa’s most promising entrepreneurs with enterprise development training, mentorship, and networking opportunities, and a platform to boost brand visibility and investment potential in Silicon Valley. Since 2018, Builders of Africa’s Future has recognised and catalysed 93 African startups.

Benefits

  • Training from the African Management Institute (AMI) of Kenya

  • Invitation to attend the 12th African Diaspora Investment Symposium. Attendees are responsible for covering all costs

Eligibility

Open to founders and executives of companies who are catalysts of developmental progress on the continent of Africa.

Primary selection criteria for builder enterprises:

  • Must be an established legal entity already in operation for 6 months or more with demonstrable early traction evidenced in growing usage, revenue, or other metrics (not a pitch or prototype). The business should have a website, a tangible digital or physical product, or a picture of more than one successful event over more than 6 months.

  • Must be an innovative approach to serve unmet needs on the continent or address its challenges. The innovation must fit within the African cultural and socioeconomic context and cannot be a clone of a Western technology or business solution without differentiation.

  • The entrepreneur must be able to attend the monthly enterprise training sessions and agree to complete 2 core modules before graduating. The training sessions are all virtual.

  • The entrepreneur must be available to attend onboarding sessions facilitated by ADN and a virtual Pitch Day at the conclusion of the programme.

Secondary considerations in selecting builders:

  • Balanced gender representation among entrepreneurs in recognition of the sizable portion of African women entrepreneurs.

  • Reasonable geographic distribution. Africa consists of 54 countries in the North, West, East, Central, and Southern regions. Builders should represent a reasonable distribution across these regions.

  • They seek impact and innovation across sectors, so they should avoid over-indexing on any one of them – e.g., multiple agro or energy companies.

  • Africa’s development is two-pronged, leveraging both for-profit and nonprofit organisations alike. Builders should represent both genres of enterprises.
Policy Center for the New South (PCNS) Junior Professional Program 2026

Applications are open for the Policy Center for the New South (PCNS) Junior Professional Program 2026. The Junior Professional Program (JPP) provides an opportunity to gain expertise in fields such as research (international relations/economics) or project management (communication, partnerships, community management, finance).

By working in a professional environment that encourages interaction between policymakers, academia, and civil society, the JPP is structured to enhance your skills and improve your employability. This two-year job contract is designed to significantly support your professional development.

Benefits

  • Multidisciplinary Expertise: Gain experience in international relations, economics, and project management.

  • Impactful Contributions: Participate in projects that influence public policies in Morocco and Africa.

  • Personalised Mentorship: Receive guidance through regular interactions as part of the programme's activities.

  • Skills Development: Develop skills in research, analysis, communication, project management, partnerships, and finance.

  • Permanent Contract Opportunity: Potential to transition to a permanent contract (CDI) based on performance, with access to competitive remuneration and benefits.

Eligibility

  • Bachelor’s (4 years) or Master’s degree (or equivalent) in international relations, economics, project management, or a related field.

  • Interest in global issues and development in Africa.

  • Strong analytical, research, and/or project management skills.

  • Fluency in at least two languages (Arabic, English, French, or others).

  • Ability to work in a team and communicate effectively.

Applications are open for the GovAI DC Summer Fellowship 2026. The DC Summer Fellowship is a three-month bipartisan opportunity designed to launch or accelerate impactful careers in American AI governance and policy. Participants deepen their understanding of the field, connect with a network of experts, and build their skills and professional profile, all while conducting a research project of their choice under expert guidance.

The programme emphasises bipartisan engagement, rigorous analysis, and practical policy relevance, with a focus on how AI policy is shaped within the US political system. They welcome a wide range of applicants, from early-career individuals interested in entering the US AI policy field to more experienced professionals looking to transition into emerging tech policy from adjacent fields.

Benefits

  • Fellows will join for three months in Washington, DC, from June 8 to August 28, 2026. Fellows will receive a stipend of $21,000, plus support for travelling to DC. This is an in-person role. While in DC, they provide fellows with lunch on weekdays and a desk in their DC office. Only candidates who have the right to work in the US are eligible to apply; they do not sponsor visas for this fellowship.

Eligibility

  • They are interested in candidates from a broad set of professional backgrounds, including those with experience in government, academia, industry, or civil society.

  • They are especially excited about candidates with relevant professional experience, research experience, or graduate study in areas of expertise relevant to US AI policy, which include – but are by no means limited to – public policy, political science, engineering, computer science, economics, biosecurity, cybersecurity, China studies, and risk management (e.g., assurance and auditing). However, there are no specific requirements for the role; they also welcome applications from promising undergraduates.

Assessment Criteria

When assessing applications, they will be looking for candidates who have the following strengths or show positive signs of being able to develop them:

  • Quality of work: The ability to produce clearly written, insightful, and even-handed research or policy advice. They are particularly excited about strong reasoning ability and clear and concise writing.

  • Relevant expertise: Skills or knowledge that are likely to be helpful for working on topics relevant to AI policy. They think that relevant expertise can take many different forms. Note that they also do not have any strict degree requirements.

  • Judgement: The ability to prioritise between different research or policy directions, and good intuitions about the feasibility of different directions.

  • Team fit: Openness to feedback, commitment to intellectual honesty and rigour, comfort in expressing uncertainty, and a serious interest in using your career to contribute to successfully navigating the transition to a world with advanced AI.

Application

The selection process has four stages:

  • Written application

  • An abstract reasoning assessment

  • Interviews and reference checks

The She Code Africa Laptop Scholarship is a transformative initiative aimed at bridging the digital divide for African women and girls pursuing technology careers. By providing brand-new laptops to selected recipients, the scholarship equips participants with the critical tools they need to fully engage in coding, software development, data science, UX/UI design, digital marketing, and other tech fields.

This initiative not only addresses the access gap to personal computing devices but also empowers women to actively participate in Africa’s rapidly evolving digital economy. It forms part of She Code Africa’s broader mission to promote gender equity in tech, ensuring that women are visible, skilled, and competitive in the continent’s technology ecosystem.

Who Should Apply:
Eligible applicants should meet the following criteria:

  • Be a female applicant passionate about technology and digital innovation.

  • Be an active member of the She Code Africa community (register here).

  • Have already embarked on a tech learning journey or career path, such as coding bootcamps, online courses, internships, or early tech roles.

  • Do not currently own a functional laptop that can support their tech learning or work.

  • Be available to receive the laptop in person, usually in Lagos, Nigeria.

Benefits of the Scholarship:
Selected recipients will receive a brand-new laptop, unlocking a range of opportunities to enhance learning, productivity, and career development:

  • Access to software development and coding tools, data analytics platforms, and design software.

  • Ability to engage in online learning, certifications, and virtual tech bootcamps.

  • Opportunities for remote internships, freelancing, and digital entrepreneurship.

  • Connection to a community of female technologists, mentorship programmes, and collaborative projects.

  • Greater career mobility and competitiveness in the technology sector.

Why This Matters:
The She Code Africa Laptop Scholarship goes beyond simply providing hardware; it addresses one of the most fundamental barriers to tech participation: access to reliable technology. Recipients gain the tools to:

  • Accelerate skill acquisition in high-demand technology fields.

  • Explore innovative solutions using digital technologies.

  • Build and launch digital projects or startups.

  • Engage in global online tech communities and collaborations, creating networks for future opportunities.

By empowering women with technology, the scholarship also contributes to closing the gender gap in Africa’s tech sector, enabling more women to take on leadership roles, mentor others, and drive innovation across industries.

How to Apply:
Interested and eligible applicants should submit their application via the She Code Africa initiatives page. Applicants who are not yet members of the She Code Africa community may be required to register first at https://shecodeafrica.org/community before applying.

Impact:
The scholarship is not just about laptops—it’s about unlocking potential, fostering inclusion, and enabling African women to thrive in tech. With access to a personal computing device, recipients can fully participate in online learning, build portfolios, and access global tech opportunities, positioning themselves as competitive contributors to Africa’s digital transformation.

Mastercard Foundation FAST Program 2026 – Up to $15,000 Funding for Alumni Entrepreneurs

  • Structured business development support for scaling, market penetration, and sustainable growth.

  • Access to a network of Mastercard Foundation alumni and partners, creating opportunities for collaboration, market exposure, and partnerships.

This integrated support package is designed to maximize business viability, leadership capacity, and community impact, helping entrepreneurs build resilient, scalable, and socially impactful enterprises.


Who Should Apply:
Applicants must:

  • Be alumni of Mastercard Foundation programmes.

  • Operate an active business currently navigating early growth, stabilization, restructuring, or post-pilot expansion.

  • Demonstrate commitment to social and economic impact.

  • Show business viability, measurable impact or growth potential, and strong ethical leadership.


Eligible Business Types:
FAST supports ventures that:

  • Create employment opportunities or contribute to local and regional economic development.

  • Address community challenges through innovative and sustainable solutions.

  • Operate at early growth, post-pilot expansion, or market adaptation stages.


Selection Criteria:
Applications will be evaluated based on:

  • Clarity and strength of the business model.

  • Growth potential and financial sustainability.

  • Measurable community or social impact.

  • Leadership capacity and ability to leverage mentorship and training opportunities effectively.

  • Alignment of FAST support with business goals and long-term impact plans.
Orange Corners Acceleration Programme for Entrepreneurs (Ghana) — Access Up to €50,000 in Funding

Young founders and growth-stage businesses in Ghana can now apply for the Orange Corners Acceleration Programme—a six-month structured support initiative that combines business development, mentorship, networking, and access to capital. The programme culminates in eligibility to access up to €50,000 in funding through the Orange Corners Innovation Fund, designed to support impactful, scalable enterprises.

What the Orange Corners Acceleration Programme Offers

The programme provides participating entrepreneurs with:

  • Structured Business Acceleration

Over 6 months, founders participate in a series of workshops and practical sessions to refine growth strategies, business models, and execution plans — including five intensive three-day workshops spaced throughout the programme.

  • Coaching, Mentoring & Masterclasses

Participants receive continuous guidance from experienced mentors and business professionals, helping them sharpen leadership skills and strengthen operational resilience.

  • Access to Finance & Funding

Entrepreneurs who successfully complete the acceleration programme can apply for finance of up to €50,000 through the Orange Corners Innovation Fund (OCIF) — a combination of partial grant and low-interest loan at approximately 5% interest for 2 years.

  • Network Connections & Ecosystem Exposure

The programme connects founders to private-sector partners, investors, industry leaders, and peer founders — helping unlock market opportunities within and beyond Ghana.

  • Post-Programme Support

Following the acceleration period, participants may benefit from up to 12 months of catalytic post-acceleration support, including in-business visits, coaching follow-ups, and strategy implementation guidance.

Who Can Apply

This programme targets entrepreneurs based in Ghana whose ventures meet the following:

  • Founder or co-founder aged between 18 and 35

  • Revenue-generating businesses with at least 24 months of commercial operations

  • Minimum USD 30,000 in annual recurring revenue

  • At least 3 full-time employees beyond the founders

  • Innovativeness and clear market impact — ideally aligned with one or more Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)

  • A business ready for expansion, scale, and structured support

  • Women founders and teams are strongly encouraged to apply
UNICEF Venture Fund for Early-Stage Startups (Equity-Free Funding Up to US$100,000)

Early-stage startups building innovative solutions for children and young people can now apply for funding through the Venture Fund. This initiative offers equity-free investments of up to US$100,000 to accelerate startups that use cutting-edge technologies and models to improve outcomes for children worldwide.

Unlike traditional venture capital, the UNICEF Venture Fund provides non-dilutive support, meaning founders retain full ownership while gaining capital, strategic guidance, and access to global networks that can help scale impact.

About the UNICEF Venture Fund

The UNICEF Venture Fund was created to support early-stage technology innovators whose solutions address key challenges affecting children — including health, education, protection, inclusion, and economic opportunity. The fund focuses on technologies with potential for wide reach and measurable impact.

By investing in emerging startups, UNICEF aims to leverage innovation to help governments, NGOs, and communities deliver better and more equitable services for children and youth around the world.

What Startups Can Receive

Selected ventures may receive:

Equity-Free Investment

  • Up to US$100,000 in non-dilutive capital to strengthen operations, product development, and scaling strategies.

Strategic Support

  • Access to UNICEF’s technical expertise and global innovation networks.

  • Guidance on impact measurement and operational scaling.

Ecosystem Connections

  • Linkages to partners, potential collaborators, and stakeholders across sectors.

  • Opportunities to showcase your solution on international platforms.

Who Should Apply

The fund targets early-stage startups that:

  • Offer technology-driven solutions with the potential to improve outcomes for children and young people.

  • Have a viable prototype or minimum viable product (MVP).

  • Demonstrate clear commitment to scaling impact and measurable outcomes.

  • Are capable of operating internationally or at scale within local contexts.

Startups working in areas such as digital health, EdTech, child protection technologies, inclusive services, and data-driven impact systems are strongly encouraged to apply.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Children and young people face complex challenges, especially in rapidly changing technological and social environments. Investing in early-stage startups that deploy innovative, tech-enabled solutions can:

  • Improve access to quality education and learning tools

  • Enhance health services and information delivery

  • Strengthen digital safety and protection systems

  • Enable economic opportunity and social inclusion

By backing entrepreneurs early, the UNICEF Venture Fund helps shift promising ideas into scalable tools that deliver real-world value for children and communities.
UNICEF Technovation Entrepreneurship Program for Young Women 2026

This structured 12-week accelerator is designed to support young women aged 19 to 24 in building and launching AI-powered startups. Programme Overview

The AI Ventures Accelerator provides practical, hands-on training that combines technical AI knowledge with entrepreneurship and startup development skills. Participants will be guided through the process of building scalable, impact-driven AI solutions that address real-world challenges.

Focus areas for solutions include education, health, climate action, financial inclusion, and broader social impact challenges.

Eligibility

  • The programme is open to young women aged 19 to 24 who are passionate about AI, technology innovation, and entrepreneurship.

  • Applicants should demonstrate a strong interest in building technology-driven solutions and a willingness to commit fully to the 12-week accelerator programme.

Benefits

Feeding the Next Billion Food Innovation Fellowship

The Feeding the Next Billion Food Innovation Fellowship is a transformative 5-day residential programme designed to empower young Nigerian innovators in the area of food value addition. The initiative is organised by Farm Central Intelligence (FarmCI), supported by UNESCO and Nestlé through the "Because You Matter” Programme, with technical expertise from Soilless Farm Lab.

The fellowship addresses two pressing challenges in Nigeria: food waste and youth unemployment. It equips participants with practical skills to convert raw agricultural produce such as vegetables, spices, and peppers into high-value, market-ready products.

Programme Overview

During the intensive residential training in Ogun State, fellows will engage in hands-on sessions covering food processing, preservation, packaging, branding, and basic business management. The curriculum is structured to provide both technical knowledge and entrepreneurial insight, helping participants build sustainable food businesses that reduce post-harvest losses and strengthen national food security.

The programme also promotes innovation, sustainability, and value-chain development within Nigeria’s food ecosystem.

Eligibility Criteria

  • Applicants must be young Nigerians with a strong interest in food innovation, agro-processing, or value addition.

  • They should demonstrate a genuine commitment to starting or growing a food-related enterprise, particularly in the processing of vegetables, spices, peppers, or similar agricultural products.

  • Applicants must be available to participate fully in the 5-day residential training programme in Ogun State.

  • They should show readiness to implement the skills acquired and contribute to reducing food waste while creating economic opportunities within their communities.

Programme Timeline

  • The fellowship begins with the application and selection phase, where interested candidates submit their applications for review. Successful applicants are notified and onboarded with detailed information about the training.

  • The core fellowship experience is a 5-day intensive residential programme hosted at Soilless Farm Lab in Ogun State. Following the training, participants enter the post-fellowship support phase, where they receive starter kits, mentorship, and business follow-up support to ensure successful implementation.

Benefits

  • Selected fellows will receive fully sponsored participation in the 5-day residential training. Accommodation, meals, and training materials will be covered in full.

  • Participants will also receive transportation support to and from the training venue in Ogun State.

  • The programme provides hands-on training and mentorship from industry experts at FarmCI and Soilless Farm Lab. Each fellow will receive a personalised starter kit containing essential tools and materials needed to immediately begin their food value addition business upon returning to their community.

  • In addition, fellows will gain networking opportunities with fellow innovators, industry professionals, and programme partners. Participants will receive a Certificate of Completion and benefit from post-programme mentorship and business development support to guide their growth journey.

NOTE: Interested applicants can submit their applications through the official application form.

Applications are open for the Google Creative Fellowship 2026. The Creative Fellowship places emerging talent on dedicated creative teams at Google, gaining exposure and hands-on experience across many of their in-house creative departments. The 6-month programme aims to provide an enriching experience while ushering in a dynamic network of creative talent with fresh perspectives and a strong “maker-first” approach.

Benefits

Eligibility

  • The programme is for emerging creative thinkers (0–5 years of experience) who have a clear point of view and passion for making things and telling stories.

Application

J-PAL’s MENA Scholars Fellowship 2026 for Researchers

Applications are open for the J-PAL’s MENA Scholars Fellowship 2026. The MENA Scholars Fellowship creates a pathway for MENA region-based researchers to build skills and experience to design and conduct randomised evaluations of poverty reduction and social development policies and programmes.

Over a two-year fellowship, selected scholars will build networks, have access to mentorship from the J-PAL affiliate network, participate in a dedicated training programme, and have the opportunity to contribute to a randomised evaluation with J-PAL MENA.

With support from Community Jameel and the Sawiris Foundation for Social Development, the MENA Scholars Fellowship supports early-career researchers and scholars interested in expanding their research portfolio to include randomised evaluations. Cohorts of MENA Scholars Fellows will help shape J-PAL MENA’s research portfolio and strengthen the local grounding and policy influence of impact evaluations across the region.

Program Components

The fellowship includes eight components:

  • Work on randomised evaluation: Fellows will work on a randomised evaluation with a J-PAL affiliate, J-PAL MENA staff (for evaluations in Egypt), and implementing partners, learning about and experiencing the lifecycle of an impact evaluation.

  • Mentoring: Fellows will be paired with a mentor from the J-PAL affiliate network, J-PAL MENA Scientific Directors, or J-PAL MENA Post-Doctoral Fellows.

  • Networking: Fellows will be members of their cohort and a growing network of MENA Scholars Fellows and will join a larger worldwide network of Scholars Fellows in other regions, in addition to having access to the J-PAL MENA network.

  • Education: Fellows will enrol in and complete two MIT MicroMasters online courses to build skills in randomised evaluation design, econometric analysis, and other related areas.

  • Capacity Building: Supplemental training targeted at the cohort of Fellows will be delivered by J-PAL MENA postdoctoral fellows and staff, focused on unique aspects of randomised evaluations in the MENA region, personal website development, and proposal writing, and to offer deeper technical training.

  • Media coverage: Fellows will have the opportunity to receive guidance from J-PAL and a public relations firm to increase media coverage of their research and policy implications.

  • Event participation: Fellows will be invited to present and participate at J-PAL MENA policy events in the region alongside J-PAL-affiliated professors and leadership.

  • Evaluation Design Project: Fellows will have the opportunity in the second year of the fellowship to design their own randomised evaluation on a topic of their interest in the MENA region and with guidance from their mentors.

Benefits

  • Each Fellow will receive a USD $15,000 stipend over the two-year fellowship period. Stipends will support fellows' participation and commitment to fellowship programme activities.

Eligibility

To be eligible for the Fellowship, candidates must meet the following requirements:

  • Candidates should be specialized in Economics and/or relevant disciplines (such as public policy, political science, public health, statistics, etc.)

    • Track 1 – PhD Students: This track is open for candidates that are currently pursuing a PhD in economics or a related discipline.

      • It includes those whose nationalities are from the MENA region and who are enrolled in PhD programmes at universities in or outside the MENA region.

    • Track 2 – Academic scholars: This track is open for candidates who have completed a PhD in economics or a related discipline and are affiliated with a MENA academic institution.

  • A high level of proficiency (fluent or native) in Arabic and English.

  • Proficiency in Stata or R is highly recommended.

  • Fellows should manifest an interest in impact evaluation in policy issues related to poverty alleviation and development in the MENA region.

  • Candidates should be able to contribute to research projects related to applied microeconomics and poverty alleviation in sectors such as education, health, and climate change, among others. Candidates have to be keen on running randomised evaluations (but do not necessarily have to have prior experience conducting them). Candidates should be motivated and organised to work independently and to deliver on their responsibilities as part of a multi-year research project team.

  • The candidate must be committed to completing all fellowship activities with J-PAL MENA for the duration of the fellowship and actively participating in opportunities afforded via the fellowship.

Application

Each candidate must complete this application form and provide a resume and a cover letter explaining their motivation behind applying for the fellowship.

Programme Overview

The SI Global Executive Programme is a premier leadership initiative designed for senior executives committed to embedding sustainability into business strategy. The programme provides participants with actionable insights from leading Swedish organisations at the forefront of the green transition, equipping executives to drive strategic transformation that is economically resilient, socially responsible, and environmentally sustainable.

Participants engage in a rigorous, project-based learning experience that combines online modules with a one-week immersive onsite session in Stockholm, Sweden, alongside a cohort of 100 global executives from 24 countries. The programme emphasizes peer learning, expert coaching, and practical application of sustainability principles directly within participants’ organisations.


Programme Structure

  • Foundation (May–June, Online): Explore the latest sustainability trends, strengthen change leadership skills, and establish peer networks.

  • Strategic Business Project (June–October): Design and implement a measurable sustainability initiative within the organisation, with expert coaching and guidance.

  • Advanced Practice Sessions (August–September, Online): Tailored, sector-specific webinars and peer dialogues, including insights from Swedish business leaders.

  • Sweden Week (12–16 October, Onsite): Field visits, workshops, and networking opportunities with leading Swedish sustainability practitioners.

  • Way Forward (November, Online): Project outcomes shared with peers and stakeholders; alumni engagement and global collaboration.

Key Benefits

  • Advanced understanding of sustainability from a business perspective

  • Tools and methodologies to integrate green transition practices into strategic planning

  • Strengthened leadership and organisational change management skills

  • Exposure to Swedish sustainability case studies and innovative business models

  • Global peer network of senior executives committed to sustainable business transformation

  • Access to the Swedish Institute Alumni Network, comprising over 20,000 leaders worldwide

Eligibility Criteria

Applicant Requirements:

  • Minimum 30 years of age (born 1996 or earlier)

  • Senior leadership role (CEO, executive, or equivalent with mandate for strategic transformation)

  • Bachelor’s degree or higher

  • Strong English proficiency

  • Residency in an eligible country (Ukrainians residing outside Ukraine are eligible)

  • Commitment to actively participate and contribute to cohort learning

Company Requirements:

  • Locally owned in an eligible country, a Swedish company represented locally, or part of a Swedish company’s supply chain

  • Operates in industries aligned with the green transition (priority: renewable energy, responsible mining, sustainable transportation, digitalisation)

  • Committed to implementing sustainable practices across the organisation

The SI Global Executive Programme equips senior leaders with the knowledge and tools to integrate sustainability into core business strategies. In an era of increasing environmental and social accountability, executives who can drive systemic change will position their organisations for long-term competitiveness, resilience, and impact.

The programme is especially relevant for leaders in emerging economies seeking to align business growth with sustainable practices, enhance operational efficiency, and strengthen organisational contribution to global sustainability standards.

For young leaders committed to entrepreneurship, social innovation, and youth impact, the Enterprising Futures Scholarship offers a unique platform to accelerate their ventures, expand their influence, and create lasting change. Participation positions scholars at the forefront of global youth leadership, providing the tools, networks, and mentorship necessary to drive enterprise and employment solutions that matter — locally, regionally, and globally.
Climate Launchpad 2026 | Global Green Business Ideas Competition for Young Entrepreneurs

ClimateLaunchpad is the world’s largest green business ideas competition, designed to empower early-stage climate innovators and aspiring entrepreneurs to transform their ideas into sustainable ventures. The programme is free and accessible to anyone with a promising cleantech idea, whether at the concept stage or just beginning their entrepreneurial journey.

Participants gain hands-on guidance, structured mentorship, and access to a global community of like-minded innovators. The programme emphasizes practical learning, helping first-time founders build, test, and refine their ideas while gaining the tools and confidence to bring their solutions to market.

All applicants also receive free access to an online green start-up mini-course, providing foundational knowledge on climate entrepreneurship, systems thinking, and venture development.


Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following conditions:

  • Have not yet started a business or be part of a company incorporated for less than two years

  • Total funding raised by the venture must be less than €200,000, including grants, angel or VC investments

  • Must not have sold products or prototypes commercially

  • Cannot have previously participated in ClimateLaunchpad or Climate-KIC Accelerator with the same idea

  • Open to students, researchers, engineers, consultants, SMEs, public sector innovators, or hybrid roles

The programme welcomes individuals or teams with ideas across key themes, including:

  • Adaptation & Resilience

  • Blue Economy

  • Circular Economies

  • Clean Energy

  • Food Systems

  • Sustainable Mobility

  • Urban Solutions

  • The Next Big Thing

Programme Structure

  1. Step 0 – Start Learning (Mini-Course): Online, self-directed learning covering climate fundamentals, systems thinking, and entrepreneurial skills.

  2. Step 1 – Build Your Idea (Boot Camp): Virtual interactive boot camp with expert trainers to strengthen your business model and pitch.

  3. Step 2 – Test and Refine: Personalized coaching to test assumptions, refine your story, and prepare for national pitching.

  4. Step 3 – Compete Internationally (Regional Finals): Top teams from each country pitch online against other strong ventures globally.

  5. Step 4 – The Global Stage (Global Grand Final in Singapore): Top-8 teams compete for visibility, mentorship, and cash prizes, alongside networking with global climate innovators.

Benefits

  • Access to a global community of climate innovators

  • Hands-on boot camp training, expert coaching, and mentorship

  • Opportunities to pitch at national, regional, and global finals

  • Full ownership of ideas and intellectual property; no equity taken

  • International exposure and visibility at a prestigious global event

  • Travel and accommodation for top winners to Singapore for the Global Grand Final


ClimateLaunchpad nurtures the next generation of green entrepreneurs, helping them transform bold ideas into viable businesses that address climate change and sustainability challenges. By combining mentorship, structured guidance, and international exposure, participants can accelerate their ventures, gain global networks, and increase their impact.

The programme also strengthens the ecosystem for cleantech innovation, fostering collaboration across industries and borders to tackle pressing environmental challenges and drive sustainable economic growth.


For aspiring climate entrepreneurs, ClimateLaunchpad 2026 offers a unique platform to turn early-stage ideas into market-ready solutions. With step-by-step guidance, global mentorship, and opportunities to pitch on the world stage, participants gain the tools, networks, and visibility needed to scale their ventures and contribute meaningfully to a sustainable future.
Africa’s Business Heroes Competition 2026 for African Entrepreneurs

Programme Overview

The Africa’s Business Heroes (ABH) Competition is one of the continent’s most prominent entrepreneurial initiatives, designed to identify, celebrate, and support African entrepreneurs who are building impactful and sustainable businesses.

Since its launch in 2019, the competition has provided a platform for founders who are not only building profitable companies but also addressing critical challenges across sectors such as healthcare, agriculture, technology, manufacturing, and social innovation.

Each year, the competition attracts over 20,000 applications from entrepreneurs across Africa. After multiple evaluation stages, 10 finalists are selected to compete in the Grand Finale for a share of $1.5 million in non-dilutive grant funding.

Beyond the financial prize, finalists benefit from mentorship, international exposure, training, and access to a powerful entrepreneurial network.

  • High-level entrepreneurial training and capacity building

  • Opportunities to pitch businesses on an international stage

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for the Africa’s Business Heroes Competition, applicants must:

  • Be the founder or co-founder actively leading the business.

  • Have the headquarters of the business located in Africa.

  • Demonstrate measurable impact, growth potential, and strong leadership.

Entrepreneurs who meet these criteria are encouraged to apply or nominate founders making meaningful contributions through their businesses.

Why Apply

Participating in the Africa’s Business Heroes Competition offers entrepreneurs the opportunity to:

  • Gain international visibility and credibility

  • Secure non-dilutive grant funding

  • Connect with investors, mentors, and strategic partners

  • Strengthen their business strategy and leadership capacity

  • Join a continent-wide community of changemakers

The programme goes beyond a traditional competition by providing founders with the support, networks, and recognition needed to scale their ventures and increase their impact across Africa.

AYuTe Africa Challenge Nigeria 2026 for Youth Led Agritech Startups

Through the challenge, selected startups receive mentorship, capacity building support, and the opportunity to compete for up to USD 40,000 in prize funding to help scale their solutions and expand their impact within Nigeria’s agricultural sector.

Why This Programme Matters

Agriculture remains one of the largest sources of employment across Africa, yet millions of smallholder farmers continue to face challenges such as limited access to markets, low productivity, climate risks, and inadequate agricultural information.

Agritech innovation is increasingly recognised as a key driver of agricultural transformation. Digital tools, data platforms, precision farming technologies, and supply chain innovations can help farmers improve yields, reduce operational costs, and access new market opportunities.

Programmes like the AYuTe Africa Challenge provide young entrepreneurs with the resources and support needed to build scalable solutions that strengthen agricultural systems while contributing to food security and rural economic development.

Who Should Apply

The challenge targets youth led agritech startups that are developing innovative solutions capable of improving the productivity and livelihoods of smallholder farmers.

Eligible applicants include:

  • Early stage agritech startups with validated solutions

  • Growth stage agritech companies seeking to expand their impact

  • Youth entrepreneurs building technology solutions for agriculture

  • Founders developing innovations that support smallholder farmer productivity and income growth

Applicants should demonstrate that their solutions have the potential to scale and create meaningful impact across agricultural value chains.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicant Requirements

Applicants should:

  • Be between 18 and 35 years old by the application deadline

  • Be the founder or co founder of the agritech enterprise

  • Hold Nigerian nationality with a valid government issued identification

  • Demonstrate innovative ideas addressing challenges in agriculture

  • Provide financial statements or evidence of venture operations

  • Show potential for partnerships and collaboration

  • Have no criminal record related to corruption

Company Requirements

The enterprise should:

  • Be registered or incorporated with relevant government agencies in Nigeria

  • Demonstrate the ability to scale and impact a large number of smallholder farmers

  • Show evidence of measurable impact on smallholder farmers

  • Demonstrate potential for job creation and poverty reduction

  • Align with national development priorities and global goals such as the Sustainable Development Goals

Innovation Requirements

The proposed agritech solution should demonstrate:

  • Clear technological innovation addressing key agricultural challenges

  • A focus on improving smallholder farmer productivity

  • Solutions that increase farmer incomes and market access

  • Technologies that reduce labour intensity in farming operations

  • Innovations that improve climate resilience in agricultural practices

  • Solutions that enhance access to agricultural information and services

Fondation Rainbow Bridge MBA Scholarships for African and Asian Women

The Fondation Rainbow Bridge Scholarship is available for women from Asian or African countries affected by natural disasters, droughts, or famines to pursue an MBA program at HEC Paris.

The Fondation Rainbow Bridge Scholarship was established in memory of Muriel Dargent, Matthieu Dargent, Iris Dargent, and Muriel’s parents, all of whom disappeared in the December 2004 tsunami.

A graduate of HEC in 1988, Muriel Dargent presented a strong role model for young women, juggling her responsibilities as wife, mother, and leader. As the Financial Director of Nihon L'Oréal in Japan, Muriel was fluent in English, Spanish and Japanese. The Fondation Rainbow Bridge will enable young women recipients to enrich their academic background by obtaining an HEC MBA, which is a Master of Business Administration degree from HEC Paris, a prestigious business school. If you are admitted after June 15th for the September intake or after November 26th for the January intake, you cannot apply for this scholarship.

Location: France

Benefits

Amount of Award: Up to two scholarships of €20,000 per year.

Eligibilities

Recipients of this scholarship are top-calibre female candidates who have been admitted to the HEC Paris MBA program (full-time only) and who can demonstrate exemplary leadership skills in one or more of the following areas:

  • Community work

  • Charity engagement,

  • Sustainable development practices.

Women who apply must come from an Asian or African country affected by a natural disaster, drought, or famine. In addition, they must demonstrate a commitment to solving some of the social and economic issues affecting their countries while working for the long-term security of the people living there, such as improving access to education, healthcare, and sustainable livelihoods.

Application Process

Only admitted candidates can apply for this scholarship.

Women who apply must come from an Asian or African country affected by a natural disaster, drought, or famine.

In addition, they must demonstrate a commitment to solving some of the social and economic issues affecting their countries while working for the long-term security of the people living there, such as improving access to education, healthcare, and sustainable resources. You cannot apply for this scholarship if you receive admission after June 15 for the September intake or after November 26 for the January intake.

Please explain, in 1,500 words, why you should be named the Fondation Rainbow Bridge Scholar at the HEC Paris MBA Program while identifying your post-MBA goals.

Applicants must submit a detailed description of their financial circumstances as well as a cash-flow forecast for the 16-month duration at HEC.
The EAUC International Green Gown Awards 2026

The International Green Gown Awards recognise, celebrate, and showcase exceptional sustainability initiatives from tertiary educational institutions around the world. Established in 2014 and supported by the UN Environment Programme (UNEP), the awards have become one of the most prestigious recognitions of leading sustainability practice in education in the world. 

The International Green Gown Awards are delivered by EAUC and are supported by Australasian Campuses Towards Sustainability (ACTS), the International Association of Universities (IAU) and the Higher Education Sustainability Initiative (HESI). The International Green Gown Award is open to tertiary education institutions outside the UK, Ireland, and Australasia. There is no limit on the number of applications or categories your institution can enter. Entry is free of charge, but if the institution can contribute, then they can give a suggested fee of $150 USD.

Awards will be given based on different categories, such as:

  • 2030 Climate Action

  • Benefiting Society

  • Creating Impact

  • Diversity, Equity and inclusion in Sustainability

  • Nature Positive

  • Next Generation Learning and Skills

  • Student Engagement

  • Sustainability Institution of the Year

Each category has its own set of criteria. It is the applicant’s responsibility to submit under the appropriate category. The award organisers will not move entries between categories, even if they feel a different category is more appropriate, unless there are exceptional circumstances.

The International Green Gown Awards application process unfolds in two stages.

In Stage 1, institutions are required to submit their initial applications by 10 April. These submissions are then assessed by the judges, and a shortlist of finalists is announced on 4 June.

In Stage 2, the shortlisted projects are contacted in early June to provide further details and supporting information. A second round of judging follows, after which the winners are revealed during the week beginning 7 September.

EAUC is the leading body for sustainability in the post-16 education sector in the UK and the Republic of Ireland. Primarily a membership body, they serve over 300 organisations while also working to change systems that enable sustainability action. The ethos of the International Green Gown Awards is to ensure the lessons and examples of best practice are shared within the sector and beyond across the world.

 Benefits

  • Enhance the sustainability profile of your institution.

  • Showcase your project, reward your team's work and inspire your community.

  • Join a unique network of International Green Gown award-winning institutions

 Eligibilities

Eligible institutions include, but are not limited to:

  • universities

  • colleges or TVETs

  • adult learning providers

  • technical or research institutions

  • learning and skills institutions
Youth Summit 2026: Future Works: Designing Jobs for the Digital Age

The World Bank Group Youth Summit returns for its 13th edition, inviting young people to engage with the most urgent issues their generation faces while bringing together thousands of participants from across the globe.

Under the theme #FutureWorks: Designing Jobs for the Digital Age, this year’s Summit will center on education and skills, entrepreneurship, and agriculture.

The Youth Summit is the World Bank Group’s largest annual event for youth (ages 18-35) worldwide. It will be held on June 11-12, 2026, featuring activities and engagements both at the World Bank Group headquarters in Washington DC and across various regions.

The mission of the Summit is to: Empower youth to explore innovative solutions to development challenges; equip youth with the tools to create and participate in impactful projects; and foster dialogue between youth, the World Bank Group, and other key global stakeholders.

The 2026 World Bank Group Youth Summit invites young changemakers, innovators, and leaders to shape a sustainable and inclusive future through bold, youth-driven solutions. Under the theme “FutureWorks: Designing Jobs for the Digital Age," this year’s summit focuses on three transformative areas: education and skills, entrepreneurship, and agriculture.

When discussing these subthemes, the Summit will focus on three fresh perspectives on innovation: technology innovation, development financing, and fragile settings. By spotlighting these areas, the event aims to amplify diverse voices, foster innovation for sustainable development, and build collaborative networks to scale impact globally.

Join them in celebrating the creativity, technology, and entrepreneurial spirit of young leaders as they redefine possibilities, impulse new job opportunities, and lead the charge for a more inclusive and resilient world.

Over the next 10 to 15 years, an estimated 1.2 billion young people in emerging markets will reach working age, while these economies are projected to generate only 400 million jobs, creating a significant employment gap. As a result, nearly 800 million young people risk being excluded from the labour market, particularly those from marginalised groups that face barriers related to gender, ethnicity, disability, and other social identities.

Against this backdrop, young people are not waiting on the sidelines. They are already shaping the future of work, designing solutions that respond to today’s labour market challenges while anticipating tomorrow’s opportunities. Across education and skills, entrepreneurship, and agriculture, youth innovators are leveraging digital technologies, new financing models, and locally grounded approaches to create pathways to decent work, productivity, and resilience. From reimagining skills development for the digital economy to building enterprises that connect innovation with livelihoods, young leaders are demonstrating how bold ideas can translate into real economic opportunity, even in the most challenging contexts.

Building on this momentum, the 2026 World Bank Group Youth Summit will serve as a global platform to elevate youth-driven solutions and connect ideas to action. By centring technology innovation, development financing, and fragile and conflict-affected settings as cross-cutting lenses, the Summit will foster collaboration among youth, governments, the private sector, and civil society to scale impact. In doing so, it aims to advance inclusive job creation and equip the next generation with the tools, networks, and resources needed to shape a more resilient and sustainable future of work.

 Benefits

  • Be part of a prestigious global platform for youth-driven impact and innovation.

  • Gain insights from industry leaders, policymakers, and development professionals, which can help you understand current trends and best practices in youth-driven impact and innovation.

  • Expand your global network with like-minded individuals and experts.

  • Join a network of 10,000+ alumni coming from 170+ countries. All delegates receive an official certificate of participation.

  • Enhance your leadership, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills in a dynamic and engaging environment.

  • By becoming a delegate, you will not only contribute to meaningful discussions but also gain valuable knowledge and connections to further your impact in the development space, such as networking opportunities with industry leaders and access to exclusive resources that can enhance your career.

 Eligibilities

  • Participants must be 18–35 years old throughout the event (born on or after 13 June 1990 and on or before 13 June 2008)

  • Open to individuals or teams of up to five members

  • All participants must be nationals of World Bank Group member countries

  • Solutions must demonstrate innovation, impact potential, and scalability

Eligible Regions: Open for nationals of World Bank Group member countries.
Megatrends Afrika Fellowship 2026 for African Researchers

Applications are open for the Megatrends Afrika Fellowship 2026. The Megatrends Afrika project is awarding three fellowships to African researchers. Megatrends Afrika is a cooperation between the German Institute for International and Security Affairs (SWP), the German Institute of Development and Sustainability (IDOS) and the Kiel Institute for the World Economy (Kiel Institute). The project conducts policy-relevant interdisciplinary research on Africa and provides policy advice to decision-makers.

Fellowships will be granted for a period of three months in fall 2026, full-time. Fellows will conduct a research project independently or, if suitable, in collaboration with project team members or other IDOS, Kiel Institute and SWP colleagues – and contribute one paper to the project’s publication series. Fellows are expected to participate in project meetings, events and other relevant activities at their host institution during their stay.

Research Topics

They are interested in proposals for a Megatrends Policy Brief that tackles an issue of relevance to one of the following research themes:

  • Peace and Security

  • Future of Democracy

  • Economic Prospects

  • Africa’s Role in the New World Order

  • Science Diplomacy and Science Cooperation

Also of interest would be research proposals on new technologies (artificial intelligence, space programmes) in African countries, international cooperation on Africa’s energy transition and critical raw materials, and the influence of digitalisation or urbanisation on political regimes in Africa.

Benefits

  • For the duration of the fellowship, fellows will reside in the city of their host institution, i.e., in Berlin (SWP), Bonn (IDOS) or Kiel (Kiel Institute). Fellows will receive a monthly grant of €2,800, an economy-class return trip between their place of residence and Germany, as well as some financial support for public transport, mobile phone credit and other ancillary costs in Germany. We will provide a letter of support and assistance in the application process for fellows who require a visa to enter Germany.

Eligibility

  • The applicant should have a completed PhD in political science, economics, social sciences, geography, international relations, or relevant area studies;

  • Originating from an African country

  • Strong research and publication track record in areas of relevance to the Megatrends Afrika project

  • Experience in scientific policy advice is an advantage

Selection Process

Fellows are selected and placed in line with the research agendas of the host institutes. Shortlisted candidates will be invited for an online interview. In choosing applicants, they will put particular emphasis on the following criteria:

  • Academic and professional merit based on the applicant’s publications and work experience;

  • Academic and policy relevance of the project;

  • Feasibility of the project during the period of the fellowship;

  • The ability of candidates to effectively communicate and make complex ideas accessible to a broad audience is crucial.

Application

Applications must contain a one-page letter of motivation, an academic CV including a list of academic publications, a sample of two recent publications, copies of relevant academic transcripts and diplomas (no translations are needed), and a written concept note containing a description of the applicant’s project. The concept note should not exceed 2,000 words and include a discussion of the project/research topic, its academic and policy relevance, feasibility and theoretical and methodological grounding. The submitted project should be based on the applicant’s existing and ongoing research agenda. Its scope and ambition have to be appropriate for a megatrend policy brief that will be finalised at the end of the fellowship.

Please email your application to Megatrends-Afrika@swp-berlin.org. Applications must be submitted as a single PDF document. Formats other than PDF (Word, zip) cannot be accepted. The deadline for the fellowship applications is 25 March 2026, 11:59 p.m. CET. Incomplete and late applications will not be considered.

Proposals are invited for the Canada-Asia Joint Research Grants 2026-2027. The Asia Pacific Foundation of Canada (APF Canada) is an independent, non-partisan, not-for-profit organization dedicated to strengthening Canada’s ties with Asia. As part of their Indo-Pacific Initiative, supported by funding from the Government of Canada, they invite proposals for joint research projects between Canadian and Indo-Pacific-based institutions.

The purpose of this initiative is to build a greater understanding of specific policy issues and policy contexts in the Indo-Pacific region and generate actionable, policy-relevant insights on Canada’s engagement with the region.

The thematic areas for this year’s call for applications include economic resilience and security; energy security; defence industrial cooperation; regional security; supply chain resilience; critical minerals; innovation; and emerging technologies (including digital governance and artificial intelligence).

Grant

  • The grant amount is set at up to C$40,000 per project.

Eligibility

  • Proposals must be jointly developed by at least one Canada-based institution and at least one institution based in the Indo-Pacific.

  • The Canadian partner must serve as the lead applicant and grant holder.

Assessment Criteria

  • Policy relevance & impact for Canadian stakeholders

  • Quality and feasibility of the research design and plan

  • Strength and complementarity of the Canada–Indo-Pacific partnership

  • Clarity of outputs and knowledge-mobilization plan

Application

Proposal Requirements (max. 5 pages)

Address the following (use clear headings):

  • Policy Focus & Relevance: What specific policy area will you address, and why does it matter for Canada? (e.g., alignment with one or more objectives of Canada’s Indo-Pacific Strategy).

  • Partnership & Roles: Who are the Canadian and Indo-Pacific partners, and what will each contribute?

  • Approach & Activities: What methods and actions will you take to gain policy-relevant insights?

  • Outputs & Mobilisation: What you will produce (e.g., policy briefs, reports, datasets, roundtables) and how you will reach decision-makers.

Annexes (not counted toward the 5-page limit):

  • Workplan & timeline (with milestones)

  • Detailed budget (with brief justifications)

To submit your application and for enquiries, contact  applications@asiapacific.ca. 

Key Dates

  • Application deadline: March 15, 2026

  • Decision notifications: April 30, 2026

  • Project completion: March 31, 2027

  • Summary report due: April 30, 2027

Iris Prize 2026 for Youth Led Nature Projects

Programme Overview
Applications are now open for the Iris Prize 2026, an international award that supports young environmental leaders who are developing projects to protect and restore nature within their communities.

The prize is organised by The Iris Project and recognises youth led initiatives addressing environmental challenges through innovative, community driven solutions. In addition to financial support, selected participants gain access to mentorship, leadership development, and a global community of young environmental changemakers.

The programme aims to strengthen grassroots environmental efforts by helping young leaders expand their projects and increase their impact in areas such as conservation, ecosystem restoration, and climate action.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Young people are playing an increasingly important role in addressing environmental issues across the world. However, many youth led initiatives face challenges accessing the funding, mentorship, and visibility required to grow their work.

The Iris Prize is designed to support promising initiatives by providing both financial resources and structured guidance. By investing in youth driven environmental projects, the programme helps strengthen local action while encouraging long term solutions for environmental sustainability.

Focus Areas

The Iris Prize supports projects that contribute to protecting and restoring natural ecosystems. Eligible initiatives may include:

  • Local conservation or biodiversity protection projects

  • Community based climate action initiatives

  • Ecosystem restoration activities such as tree planting or habitat restoration

  • Nature based solutions that promote environmental sustainability

  • Youth driven environmental education or advocacy initiatives

Projects that respond directly to environmental challenges within their communities are particularly encouraged.

Eligibility Requirements

To apply for the Iris Prize, applicants must meet the following criteria:

Prize Categories and Funding

The programme offers three funding tiers designed to support projects at different stages:

Seed Prize – $5,000

Stem Prize – $10,000

Iris Prize – $15,000
The main award given to more established youth led projects with strong potential to expand their environmental impact and reach.

Additional Benefits

  • Leadership development and project management training

  • Access to a network of young environmental leaders and sustainability advocates

These benefits help participants build stronger projects while connecting with a broader community focused on protecting the environment.
