Star Mentors Lead ECHO Creator Accelerator

Woof Studios Africa has announced the distinguished lineup of mentors and faculty members for the ECHO Creator Accelerator, an initiative designed to help African digital creators turn their platforms into sustainable businesses. The programme, already attracting strong interest within the creator community, features some of the continent’s most influential storytellers, entrepreneurs, and media professionals.

Free applications for the accelerator are currently open via www.echoaccelerator.com, with the deadline set for 11:59 PM WAT on March 22, 2026.

Often described as “the Harvard of the Creator Economy,” the ECHO programme is built to close the gap between online popularity and long-term business sustainability. The initiative will bring together a powerful network of creators and industry experts to help participants convert digital influence into scalable, global ventures.

The accelerator is scheduled to begin in early April 2026, featuring immersive learning sessions in Lagos alongside virtual participation options. Through structured training and mentorship, selected creators will learn how to transition from simply producing content to building professionally managed media brands.

According to Adetutu Laditan, Founder and Creative Director of Woof Studios Africa, the programme is focused on redefining the value creators generate.

“ECHO is about shifting the narrative from being ‘internet famous’ to becoming ‘industry foundational.’ We want African creators to build systems that allow them to truly own the value they create instead of depending solely on platform algorithms.”

A Powerhouse Faculty of Creators and Industry Leaders

Participants admitted into the programme will receive mentorship from a carefully selected group of creators and industry leaders who have built successful platforms across entertainment, media, and digital entrepreneurship.

The confirmed faculty includes:

Industry Leaders

  • Award-winning actress and producer Ruth Kadiri

  • Actor and creator Elozonam Ogbolu

  • Filmmaker Dammy Twitch

  • Media strategist Seyi Ekisola

Content and Media Experts

  • Global travel filmmaker Tayo Aina

  • Creator and digital entrepreneur Tomike Adeoye

  • Brand communications expert Adebola Williams

Education and Knowledge Creators

  • Digital health advocate Chinonso Egemba (Aproko Doctor)

  • Storyteller Ibidunmi Oladayo

Growth and Audience Specialists

  • Talent development expert Miss Tennie

  • Food storyteller Ify Mogekwu (Ify’s Kitchen)

Together, these mentors will guide participants through practical strategies for building successful digital brands in both African and international markets.

The Three-Pillar Framework for Creator Success

The ECHO curriculum is built around a three-pillar framework designed to help creators transition into creative entrepreneurs and media CEOs.

  1. Monetization Frameworks Creators will learn how to move beyond dependence on brand partnerships by building diversified income streams. This includes strategies for digital products, licensing, partnerships, and other sustainable revenue models.

  1. Business Structure This pillar focuses on professionalizing creative work by teaching creators how to set up operational systems, legal structures, and organized workflows that support long-term growth.

  1. Global Positioning Participants will also learn how to position their content for international audiences while maintaining cultural authenticity, helping African creators expand their reach beyond local markets.

Limited Slots for the First Cohort

The inaugural ECHO accelerator cohort will be highly selective. From all submitted applications, only 15 creators will be chosen, ensuring that participants receive deep mentorship and hands-on support.

To qualify, applicants must already demonstrate a growing digital presence, including:

  • At least 1,000 subscribers on YouTube, or

  • 10,000 followers on Instagram or TikTok

Interested creators can apply through the official programme website before the application deadline.

Application Details

  • Deadline: 11:59 PM WAT, March 22, 2026

The ECHO Creator Accelerator represents a growing effort to equip African digital creators with the skills, systems, and global mindset needed to transform influence into lasting impact.
GET Scholarship 2026 Opens for Nigerian Students at Amadeus University

Applications are now open for the Grooming Endowment Trust (GET) Scholarship 2026, a fully funded scholarship designed to support academically outstanding Nigerian students pursuing undergraduate studies at Amadeus University.

The scholarship provides comprehensive financial support covering tuition, service charges, and accommodation, enabling qualified students to pursue university education without the financial pressures often associated with higher education.

Scholarship Overview

The GET Undergraduate Scholarship aims to expand access to quality education by supporting talented Nigerian students who demonstrate strong academic potential.

A total of 20 full scholarships will be awarded to successful applicants. Each scholarship covers:

  • Full tuition fees for the duration of the degree programme

  • University service fees

  • Accommodation costs for on-campus living

The scholarship remains valid for the entire duration of the student’s degree programme, provided the recipient maintains the required academic performance.

Why This Matters

Access to higher education remains a significant challenge for many talented Nigerian students due to rising tuition and living costs. Scholarship programmes such as the GET Scholarship play a vital role in supporting deserving students by removing financial barriers to quality education.

By investing in academically strong students, the initiative helps nurture future professionals and leaders who can contribute to national development and economic growth.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the GET Scholarship 2026, applicants must:

  • Be Nigerian citizens

  • Demonstrate strong academic performance

  • Select Amadeus University as their preferred institution before applying

  • Be seeking admission into an undergraduate degree programme

  • Meet all admission and documentation requirements listed on the application portal

Application Requirements

Applicants will typically be required to submit the following documents:

  • WASSCE or NECO results, or recent academic term results

  • A personal statement explaining why they deserve the scholarship

  • An academic statement outlining their interest in their chosen field of study

  • JAMB application form indicating Amadeus University among their selected institutions

  • At least one recommendation letter, usually from a teacher or academic mentor

Selection Process

The scholarship selection process generally includes the following stages:

  1. Online application submission

  2. Initial screening and shortlisting of candidates

  3. Interviews with shortlisted applicants

  4. Final selection and notification of successful candidates

  5. Submission of supporting documents upon acceptance of the scholarship offer

Interested applicants can learn more and apply through the official scholarship page

UNESCO Prize for Girls’ and Women’s Education 2026 Opens for Nominations (Up to $50,000 Award)

Nominations are now open for the UNESCO Prize for Girls’ and Women’s Education 2026, a global award that recognises outstanding initiatives advancing access to education for girls and women around the world.

Established by UNESCO and funded by the Government of the People’s Republic of China, the prize highlights innovative projects and programmes that significantly improve educational opportunities for girls and women and contribute to improving their quality of life.

Launched in 2016, the award is the only UNESCO prize specifically dedicated to promoting girls’ and women’s education. Each year, the prize honours organisations or individuals whose work demonstrates measurable impact and innovation in expanding access to education.

Award

Two laureates are selected annually, with each recipient receiving a $50,000 prize to support and expand their work in advancing girls’ and women’s education.

Eligibility Criteria

Candidates cannot apply directly for the prize. Instead, nominations must be submitted by:

  • Governments of UNESCO Member States

  • Non-governmental organisations (NGOs) that are in official partnership with UNESCO

Each eligible nominating body may submit up to three nominations for individuals, institutions, or organisations that have made strong and innovative contributions to improving girls’ and women’s education.

Self-nominations are not permitted.

Nomination Process

Nominations must be submitted through the official UNESCO online nomination platform and can be completed in English or French.

Applications may be submitted by:

  • A National Commission for UNESCO or the Permanent Delegation of the relevant Member State

  • An NGO in official partnership with UNESCO

Nominations submitted by a National Commission must be validated by the Permanent Delegation of the Member State to UNESCO. For NGOs, nominations must be approved by the organisation’s headquarters, as submissions from local branches or affiliated offices will not be accepted.

ECOWAS ROGEAP Solar Business Plan Competition 2026 for West African MSMEs

Applications are now open for the ROGEAP Regional Solar Business Plan Competition 2026, an initiative designed to support renewable energy entrepreneurs and micro, small, and medium-sized enterprises (MSMEs) developing innovative solar solutions across West Africa.

The competition is implemented under the Regional Off-Grid Electricity Access Project (ROGEAP) through the ECOWAS Centre for Renewable Energy and Energy Efficiency (ECREEE). It aims to promote entrepreneurship and innovation in the off-grid solar sector while expanding access to reliable and sustainable energy in underserved communities across the region.

About the Competition

The ROGEAP Solar Business Plan Competition targets entrepreneurs and MSMEs working on solutions that improve electricity access, particularly in rural and off-grid communities.

Participants will present business models focused on delivering affordable and scalable solar energy solutions capable of supporting sustainable development and economic growth across ECOWAS member states.

Who Should Apply

  • Startups developing off-grid solar products or services

  • Entrepreneurs building clean energy innovations

The African Volunteer Health Corps (AVoHC) invites applications for the 2026 Kofi Annan Scholars Program, a continental initiative designed to rapidly deploy skilled African public health professionals in support of Member States during health emergencies and other priority public health interventions. The program strengthens Africa’s collective capacity to prevent, detect, and respond to health threats in a coordinated and timely manner.

About the Program

The Kofi Annan Scholars Program (PHSP) places experienced African public health experts—including those from the African Diaspora—into time-bound, results-oriented deployments within Ministries of Health, National Public Health Institutes (NPHIs), and other strategic health institutions. Scholars provide technical assistance, support institutional transformation, and deliver clearly defined outputs aligned with Africa CDC priorities.

Since 2012, the African Diaspora has been recognized as the sixth region of the African Union. The Scholars Program leverages diaspora expertise to advance Africa’s health security, institutional resilience, and sustainable health financing.

Opportunity: Strategy Development Expert

Africa CDC is establishing a roster of qualified Strategy Development Experts under the AVoHC mechanism. Inclusion in the roster does not constitute a staff appointment, but enables paid, short-term deployments based on program needs, funding availability, and agreed deliverables. Scholars engaged through this mechanism will receive remuneration and benefits in accordance with AU rules, reflecting the seniority, expertise, and outputs required.

Key Responsibilities

  • Conduct situational analyses and capability mapping to guide national or institutional strategies.

  • Identify policy, operational, and resource gaps, and propose solutions.

  • Lead or contribute to drafting strategies aligned with AU and country priorities.

  • Facilitate strategic planning consultations and co-creation workshops.

  • Develop strategic frameworks, roadmaps, implementation tools, and monitoring mechanisms.

  • Produce high-quality technical documents, policy briefs, and guidance.

  • Support resource mobilization, partner coordination, and integration of strategic initiatives.

  • Contribute to monitoring, evaluation, and learning (MEL) systems to track impact.

Academic & Professional Requirements

  • Master’s or Doctoral degree in Public Health, Health Policy, Health Systems Management, Strategic Planning, or related field.

  • Minimum 7 years of experience in strategic planning, national policy development, or institutional reform in public health.

  • Proven experience leading or contributing to strategy development processes.

  • Strong knowledge of health systems and policy environments across Africa.

  • Experience working with Ministries of Health, NPHIs, development partners, and civil society.

  • Familiarity with Africa CDC initiatives and AU strategic frameworks (e.g., Agenda 2063) is an advantage.

  • Excellent strategic writing and policy communication skills.

Required Skills

Functional Skills:

  • Advanced policy analysis, strategic planning, and institutional development.

  • Ability to translate technical insights into actionable strategy documents.

  • Strong facilitation and multi-stakeholder management skills.

Personal Abilities:

  • High resilience and adaptability in dynamic or resource-limited settings.

  • Strong emotional intelligence and cultural sensitivity.

  • Ability to work independently while collaborating effectively with teams.

  • Commitment to service, integrity, and impact.

Language Requirement:

  • Proficiency in at least one AU working language (English, French, Arabic, Portuguese, Spanish, Swahili).

  • Knowledge of additional AU working languages is an advantage.

Tenure & Deployment

  • Scholars are deployed on an on-call basis, based on requests from Member States.

  • Deployment duration is determined in consultation with member state authorities.

Benefits & Remuneration

During deployment, scholars are entitled to:

  • Stipend for the duration of the assignment.

  • Economic-class round-trip travel and deployment-related expenses.

  • Health insurance during deployment.

  • Accommodation allowance if deployed outside the city of residence.

  • Communications, incidentals, and risk allowances.

  • Certificate recognizing contribution at the end of deployment.

Note: Membership in AVoHC does not include monetary incentives; financial benefits are applicable only during actual deployment.

How to Apply

For more information and to submit your application, visit the Official AVoHC Kofi Annan Scholars Program Webpage.

Application Deadline: April 2, 2026

AstraZeneca Young Health Programme (YHP) Impact Fellowship 2026

AstraZeneca is now accepting applications for the 2026 Young Health Programme (YHP) Impact Fellowship! This Fellowship seeks passionate, driven young leaders to create meaningful change in youth health and well-being.

Program Focus Areas

Selected Fellows will implement projects that address one of the following:

  1. Advancing Health Equity

  • Duration: 6–12 months, starting after October 2026 and ending by December 2027.

  • Ineligible projects: political/religious advocacy, grants to other organizations, medical courses, scholarships, medical treatment/promotion, projects benefiting AstraZeneca commercially, projects exceeding 12 months, projects already completed or starting before November 2026, or projects funded more than 50% by other sources.

Since its launch in 2024, GFiC has accelerated the work of 57 human rights leaders. In 2026, 45 Fellows will be selected to join the program, which fosters collaboration, shared learning, and long-term impact. By 2030, GFiC aims to empower a network of 300+ human rights leaders active in nearly 100 countries, contributing to legislative, legal, and grassroots change affecting billions of people.

Location

  • Online (via Zoom)

Program Benefits

  • Curriculum: Access to a structured online program with bi-monthly Zoom sessions.

  • Cohort: Join a global community of human rights leaders.

  • Connections: Gain access to GFiC’s network of advisors and experts for mentorship and guidance.

  • Coaching: Individualized support from experienced coaches throughout the program.

  • Grant Opportunities: Eligible to compete for grants to advance your projects upon successful completion.

  • Certification: Receive a certificate of completion from GFiC.

Eligibility

  • Aged 25–35 (exceptions possible)

  • Minimum 3 years of experience in human rights advocacy, activism, community organizing, or social entrepreneurship

  • Demonstrated engagement in gender justice or related areas

  • Proficiency in English

  • Ability to commit time and fully participate in program expectations

Eligible Regions: Open globally

Application Process

  • Apply online via the ‘APPLY NOW’ link
The Nature Awards Inspiring Women in Science 2026

The Inspiring Women in Science Awards celebrate and support outstanding achievements by women in STEM while recognizing initiatives that encourage girls and young women to pursue and remain in science, technology, engineering, mathematics, and medicine (STEM) careers worldwide. The awards honor individuals, organizations, departments, and project teams demonstrating exceptional vision, innovation, and measurable impact in advancing gender equity in STEM.

Award Categories

  1. Science Outreach

    • Recognizes initiatives that inspire girls and young women to engage with STEM education or improve retention and career progression of women in STEM.

    • Eligible entries include mentorship programs, educational access initiatives, institutional reform efforts, leadership development, or interdisciplinary approaches with measurable impact.

  2. Scientific Achievement

    • Honors women who have made outstanding contributions to scientific discovery and have supported efforts to inspire the next generation of scientists.

    • Applicants are expected to demonstrate potential to develop and lead independent research groups and may include individuals who have overcome significant personal or professional challenges.

Prizes and Benefits

  • Two winners receive US$50,000 each.

  • Two runners-up receive US$5,000 each.

  • Invitation to the award ceremony in London or New York with travel and accommodation covered.

  • Media coverage on nature.com and mentoring opportunities via The Estée Lauder Companies.

  • Shortlisted applicants gain visibility on the Nature Awards website and access to an exclusive coaching and networking community.

Eligibility

Science Outreach:

  • Open to organizations, departments, individuals (any gender), or project teams.

  • Initiatives must have:

    • Encouraged girls or young women to engage with STEM subjects, or

    • Increased retention of women in STEM careers with tools supporting career progression.

  • Entries must demonstrate exceptional vision and measurable impact.

  • Interdisciplinary initiatives are welcome.

  • One spokesperson must be nominated to represent the entry at the award ceremony.

Scientific Achievement:

  • Applicants must self-identify as women (inclusive of cisgender women, trans women, and genderqueer/non-binary individuals identifying as women).

  • Demonstrated exceptional and measurable contributions to scientific discovery.

  • Potential to develop and lead an independent research group.

  • Evidence of inspiring or supporting the next generation of scientists.

  • Must hold a PhD, MD, or equivalent doctorate obtained within the last 10 years (eligibility window can be extended for career breaks such as maternity leave).

  • Must hold a non-tenured position and work in STEM disciplines.

  • Interdisciplinary research components are accepted.

Eligible Regions: Open globally

Application Process

  • Apply via the “Apply Now” link.

The Commonwealth Distance Learning Master’s Scholarships are now open for applications! Offered by the Commonwealth Scholarship Commission (CSC) in the UK, this programme enables talented postgraduate students from developing Commonwealth countries to access high-quality UK Master’s education while remaining in their home countries.

Program Overview

The scholarships aim to develop future leaders and innovators who can contribute to the sustainable development of their home countries. Through flexible online and blended learning formats, scholars gain UK-accredited degrees without relocating, while benefiting from international collaboration and the global Commonwealth alumni network.

The program supports six CSC Development Themes:

  • Science and Technology for Development

  • Improving Population Health, Health Systems, and Capacity

  • Promoting Innovation and Entrepreneurship

  • Strengthening Peace, Security & Governance

  • Strengthening Resilience & Response to Crises

  • Access, Inclusion & Opportunity

Location

  • Online

Benefits

  • Fully funded tuition fees for selected UK Master’s programmes

  • Access to a UK-accredited degree without relocating

  • Connection to the global Commonwealth alumni network

Eligibility

Applicants must:

  • Be a citizen or refugee of an eligible Commonwealth country, or a British Protected Person

  • Be permanently resident in a developing Commonwealth country

  • Hold a first degree of at least upper second class (2:1) standard by September 2026 (lower qualifications considered with relevant experience in some cases)

  • Demonstrate financial need to study the programme without the scholarship

Eligible Regions: Bangladesh, Belize, Botswana, Cameroon, Dominica, Eswatini, Fiji, Gabon, Ghana, Grenada, Guyana, India, Jamaica, Kenya, Kiribati, Lesotho, Malawi, Malaysia, Maldives, Mauritius, Montserrat, Mozambique, Namibia, Nauru, Nigeria, Pakistan, Papua New Guinea, Rwanda, Saint Helena, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Samoa, Sierra Leone, Solomon Islands, South Africa, Sri Lanka, Tanzania, The Gambia, Togo, Tonga, Tuvalu, Uganda, Vanuatu, Zambia

Application Process

Apply online via the Apply Now link and upload the following documents:

  • Proof of citizenship or refugee status in an eligible Commonwealth country (passport or national ID)

  • Full transcripts of all higher education qualifications (with certified translations if not in English)

References from at least two individuals on institutional letterhead or official email

The Petty Traders Grant Support Programme is now open for applications! This initiative is designed to empower aspiring and existing small-scale business owners across Nigeria by providing seed capital to start or expand their enterprises.

Program Overview

The programme offers financial assistance ranging from ₦10,000 to ₦100,000 to help petty traders generate sustainable income. It is ideal for individuals involved in buying and selling goods or offering basic services, as well as existing traders seeking recapitalization or support to grow their businesses.

The initiative is led by Youth Empowerment Nigeria (YEN), a project committed to bridging youth and community resources, promoting civic engagement, capacity building, and empowering young Nigerians to contribute meaningfully to their communities.

Location

  • Nigeria

Benefits

  • Seed capital between ₦10,000 – ₦100,000 to start or grow a business

  • Specifically tailored for petty traders requiring minimal start-up capital

  • Simple application process with minimal documentation

  • Open to all Nigerians within the required age bracket, with or without CAC registration

  • Supports both aspiring entrepreneurs and existing business owners

Eligibility

Applicants must:

  • Be 18–55 years old

  • Be an entrepreneur or aspiring entrepreneur

  • Own a business or have a viable business idea/initiative

  • Possess a valid National Identification Number (NIN slip)

  • Provide a contact person

  • Have a verifiable residential address in Nigeria

  • Hold a valid 10-digit bank account

  • Reside in Nigeria

  • Submit a completed application form

Eligible Regions: Nigeria

Application Process

  • Apply online via the APPLY NOW link

The Allan Gray Fellowship Programme is now open for applications! This prestigious fellowship seeks young achievers transitioning into university who are committed to making a meaningful impact—not only in their own lives but also within their communities, cities, and countries. Fellows receive needs-based funding for university studies and access to comprehensive development support aimed at cultivating an entrepreneurial mindset.

Program Overview

The Allan Gray Fellowship focuses on nurturing entrepreneurship by equipping candidates to:

  • Create value through responsible entrepreneurship

  • View entrepreneurship as a viable career path

  • Transform ideas into viable business opportunities

  • Understand the value proposition of the Allan Gray Orbis Foundation and E Squared, the Foundation’s venture funding partner

  • Embrace a culture of community, collaboration, and ethical leadership

Fellows receive mentorship and guidance from:

  • A Personal Development Officer to support their transition into university life

  • An Entrepreneurial Leadership Officer to challenge and develop their entrepreneurial skills

  • An Industry Mentor to provide practical insights into entrepreneurial traits and concepts in the workplace

The Allan Gray Orbis Foundation invests in the education and development of youth with high entrepreneurial potential across Southern Africa, aiming to create empowered, ethical leaders who drive meaningful job creation and long-term societal impact.

Location

  • South Africa

Benefits

Academic & Financial Support

  • Applicants from households with a combined annual income ≤ R1 million: full funding, including university tuition, accommodation, meals, textbooks, tutoring allowance, and a monthly stipend

  • Applicants from households with a combined annual income > R1 million: needs-based funding

  • Funding details communicated after the camp stage

Entrepreneurial Support

  • Individual mentorship from Foundation staff and business mentors

  • Access to the Foundation’s online entrepreneurial curriculum

  • Participation in annual entrepreneurship conferences, seminars, and workshops

  • Connection to a community of like-minded peers

  • Access to business incubation and venture funding via E Squared (subject to business idea viability)

Eligibility

Applicants must:

  • Achieve ≥60% in Pure Mathematics or ≥80% in Mathematical Literacy (Grade 11 final results)

  • Achieve a minimum average of 70% across Grade 11 final results (excluding Life Orientation)

  • Be currently in Grade 12 during the year of application

  • Be under 21 years of age

  • Be a citizen of South Africa, Namibia, Botswana, or Eswatini

  • Intend to study towards a degree in Commerce, Science, Engineering, Law, Humanities, Arts, or Health Sciences (excluding Medicine, Veterinary Science, and Dentistry) at WITS, UJ, UCT, NMU, RU, UWC, SU, UP, UFS, UKZN, or TSiBA

  • Demonstrate entrepreneurial flair and passion

Eligible Regions: South Africa, Namibia, Botswana, and Eswatini

Application Process

  1. Submit the online application form and supporting documents by April 30, 2026

  2. Successful candidates will be invited for an interview in July 2026

  3. Interviewed candidates will attend a selection camp in September 2026

Applicants are encouraged to allocate at least 2 days to complete the application to ensure accuracy and completeness.
Youth to the Front Fund – “The Creatives” Frontliners 2026

Applications are now open for the We Are Family Foundation (WAFF) Youth to the Front Fund: “The Creatives” Frontliners 2026. This program supports young creative leaders under 30 whose work centers BIPOC communities and actively challenges systemic racism, inequality, and injustice.

Program Overview

The Youth to the Front Fund recognizes creatives as cultural leaders, storytellers, and changemakers. The program empowers young artists to drive community healing, cultural preservation, and systemic change through innovative, socially engaged creative work.

Eligible Creative Fields

Applicants must be founding leaders under 30 working in one or more of the following areas:

  • Visual & Material Arts: Painting, sculpture, photography, film, design, fashion, craft

  • Performance & Embodied Practice: Dance, theatre, live performance, ritual-informed expression

  • Music & Sound: Composition, production, DJing, sound art, audio storytelling

  • Writing & Literary Arts: Poetry, prose, spoken word, journalism, publishing, archival work

  • Film, Media & Cultural Documentation: Documentary, short films, community journalism, archival projects

  • Design, Architecture & Spatial Practice: Architecture, urban design, public art, placemaking

  • Digital & Creative Technology: Coding, immersive media, gaming, XR/AR/VR, web-based art

  • Creative Changemaking & Cultural Strategy: Curation, cultural organizing, culinary arts, civic engagement

  • Holistic Healing, Community Spaces & Sport: Wellness, restorative healing, youth sports, shared spaces


 Eligibility Criteria

To apply, you must:

  • Be under 30 years old

  • Be a founding leader of a creative initiative or collective

  • Center your work on BIPOC communities

  • Actively challenge systemic racism, inequity, and injustice

Application Process

  • Apply online via WAFF’s official portal

  • Submit portfolio, project description, and supporting materials

  • Applications are evaluated based on impact, creativity, community engagement, and cultural preservation

Why This Matters

This fund emphasizes:

  • Community Healing: Using art and creative practice to restore and empower communities

  • Cultural Preservation: Treating heritage as living knowledge, not nostalgia

  • Joy as Resistance: Harnessing creativity to nurture resilience and connection

  • Equity & Justice: Supporting leaders who dismantle systemic barriers

The Hauwa Ojeifo Scholarship, offered by One Young World, supports young leaders from Africa, Asia, and the Middle East who are driving transformative solutions to urgent challenges affecting women and girls. Selected scholars receive full access to the One Young World Summit 2026, lifelong ambassador membership, leadership development opportunities, and potential grant funding of up to USD 25,000, along with travel, accommodation, and meals.

Overview

The scholarship is designed to empower emerging changemakers committed to advancing the rights, health, and wellbeing of women and girls globally. Delivered in partnership with the Hauwa Ojeifo Global Impact Fund for Women & Girls, the program equips young leaders with the skills, knowledge, and global platform to create measurable impact across multiple sectors.

Priority Focus Areas

Applicants should demonstrate leadership in initiatives addressing one or more of the following:

  • Mental health, including neurodivergence and developmental differences

  • Domestic and sexual violence prevention and intervention

  • Disability rights and the rights of older people

  • Gynaecological health and sexual and reproductive health and rights (SRHR)

  • Maternal and child health

  • Issues affecting the girl child, including FGM, adolescent pregnancy, and child marriage

  • Prevention and management of non-communicable diseases

Programme Benefits

  • One Young World Summit 2026: Full access to the Summit in Cape Town, South Africa (3–6 November 2026)

  • Global Ambassador Network: Lifelong membership in the One Young World Ambassador Community

  • Leadership Development: Participation in global leadership and action accelerator programs

  • Funding Opportunity: Potential grants of up to USD 25,000 for eligible initiatives

  • Travel & Logistics: Economy return flights, hotel accommodation, daily meals, and local transport during the Summit

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be aged 18–30 at the time of the Summit (exceptional candidates above 30 may be considered)

  • Reside and create impact in Africa, Asia, or the Middle East

  • Hold a leadership role or champion an initiative with measurable impact aligned with the scholarship themes

  • Actively contribute to initiatives demonstrating commitment to one or more priority focus areas

  • Be willing to collaborate and engage with the One Young World community

How to Apply

  1. Submit your application through the official One Young World portal.

  2. Provide evidence of:

    • Your leadership role and measurable impact of your initiative

    • Alignment with one or more priority focus areas

    • Commitment to collaboration within the global One Young World network

  3. Longlisted candidates may be asked to provide references and additional documentation.

  4. Submit by the application deadline: March 27, 2026

Selection Process

  • Applications are evaluated based on demonstrated impact, leadership potential, and alignment with the priority focus areas

  • Commitment to engagement within the One Young World community is considered

Sixteen scholars will be selected for the 2026 cohort

The Powerful Social Enterprise Startup Program 2026 is now accepting applications! This initiative provides seed funding and mentorship to young entrepreneurs building impact-driven ventures. Designed for early-stage founders, the program supports participants to scale their social enterprises with both financial backing and expert guidance.

Program Overview

  • Program Name: Powerful Social Enterprise Startup Program 2026

  • Focus: Supporting social entrepreneurs with funding, mentorship, and capacity-building

  • Target Audience: Early-stage founders working on enterprises with measurable social impact

  • Application Status: Open (March 2026)

The program aims to empower innovators who are tackling pressing social challenges across sectors such as education, health, environment, and inclusion.

Benefits

Participants gain access to:

  • Seed Funding: Financial support to launch or scale their social enterprise

  • Mentorship: Guidance from experienced entrepreneurs, investors, and social impact leaders

  • Networking Opportunities: Connection to a global community of innovators and changemakers

  • Capacity Building: Training in business development, fundraising, and impact measurement

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants should meet the following criteria:

  • Be an early-stage founder of a social enterprise

  • Demonstrate a clear social impact mission (education, health, environment, inclusion, or similar)

  • Show commitment to scaling the enterprise sustainably

  • Be willing to participate in mentorship, training sessions, and program activities

Application Process

  1. Complete the online application form via the program portal

  2. Submit supporting documents, such as a business plan, pitch deck, or evidence of social impact

  3. Shortlisted candidates may be invited for interviews or pitch sessions

  4. Selected participants will receive seed funding and mentorship and join the program

Considerations

  • Competitive Process: The program is highly competitive; applicants should prepare a strong, well-documented proposal

  • Funding Scale: Seed funding may be limited, requiring additional fundraising for growth

  • Time Commitment: Participation requires engagement in mentorship and training, which may affect other business activities
Katapult Africa Program 2026 – Accelerating Climate Tech in Africa

Katapult Africa 2026 is a three-month acceleration program designed to support early-stage climate tech startups across Africa. The program equips participants with the knowledge, mentorship, and investor connections needed to scale their businesses, achieve impact, and reach commercialization milestones.

Program Overview

  • Organizer: Katapult VC

  • Program Duration: 3 months

  • Focus: Growth, investor readiness, and impact management for climate tech startups

  • Target Audience: Early-stage startups in Africa with scalable, revenue-oriented business models

Participants benefit from:

  • Intensive workshops and learning sessions

  • Mentorship from industry experts and investors

  • Access to a pan-African network of stakeholders

  • Guidance on scaling impact, commercialization, and fundraising

Focus Areas / Eligible Sectors

Katapult Africa supports startups with solutions in the following impact areas:

  • Precision Agriculture

  • Waste Management

  • Clean Energy

  • Clean Mobility

  • Climate Fintech

  • Carbon Monitoring & Reduction

Eligible startups should be impact-driven, led by experienced teams, and have clear paths to commercialization or early revenue.

Eligibility Criteria

To apply, startups must:

  • Be early-stage climate tech ventures based in Africa

  • Have scalable business models with potential for significant environmental impact

  • Be led by teams with relevant experience and technical expertise

  • Demonstrate a clear path to commercialization or early revenue generation

Program Benefit

Participants will gain:

  • Expert mentorship and technical guidance

  • Structured workshops to improve business and investment readiness

  • Networking with investors, industry leaders, and peer startups

  • Tools and frameworks for measuring and managing impact

  • Opportunities to attract investment and scale operations across Africa

Eligible Regions

  • Open to startups operating anywhere in Africa

How to Apply

  • Apply online via the Katapult Africa website

  • Submit details about your team, business model, and impact focus

  • Shortlisted startups may be invited to pitch and participate in program sessions

Applications are now open for the NNPC–Seplat Youth Entrepreneurship Program (YEP) 2026, a practical capacity-building initiative designed to equip young Nigerians with entrepreneurial skills, mentorship, and access to business funding opportunities.

This programme is a collaboration between the Nigerian National Petroleum Corporation (NNPC) and Seplat Energy, delivered through the Seplat Growth Academy.

About the Program
YEP 2026 empowers young Nigerians with the knowledge, tools, and resources to start and grow sustainable businesses. The programme combines hands-on training, mentorship, and funding support, providing participants with practical experience in business development, financial literacy, innovation, and enterprise management.

Program Benefits
Selected participants will receive:

  •  Hands-on entrepreneurship training

  •  Access to business funding opportunities

  •  Certificate of completion

  •  Mentorship from industry experts

  •  Skills in business planning, growth, and sustainability

  •  Access to a network of young entrepreneurs and professionals

Who Should Apply
The programme is open to:

Applications are now open for the WTO Young Trade Leaders Programme 2026, a global initiative designed to engage young people in international trade, policy dialogue, and global economic development.

Organised by the World Trade Organization (WTO), the programme brings together emerging leaders from across the world to deepen understanding of trade and its impact on economies, businesses, and society.

About the Programme
The WTO Young Trade Leaders Programme aims to build a dynamic global network of young individuals passionate about international trade and sustainable development.

Selected participants will serve as WTO Young Trade Leaders for one year—contributing ideas, engaging in global conversations, and promoting awareness of trade-related issues within their communities. Participants will also contribute to discussions on the future of the global trading system and ongoing WTO reforms.

Programme Benefits
Selected Young Trade Leaders will:

  • Gain in-depth knowledge of international trade and WTO operations

  • Receive mentorship and guidance from WTO experts

  • Create and publish content (articles, blogs, podcasts, social media) on trade topics

  • Participate in a training programme in Geneva, Switzerland

  • Engage in the WTO Public Forum and other global discussions

  • Receive support to organise trade-related events in their home countries

Note: This is a leadership and advocacy opportunity and does not offer a salary. However, selected participants may be invited to Geneva for key programme events.

Eligibility Criteria
Applicants must:

  • Be between 18 and 28 years old

  • Be a citizen of a WTO member country (Nigeria is eligible)

  • Have a strong interest in international trade and global development

  • Demonstrate strong written and spoken English skills

  • Show leadership potential and ability to engage in dialogue and advocacy

  • Not be currently affiliated with the WTO or have close relatives working there

What You’ll Do as a Young Trade Leader
Participants will:

  • Advocate for inclusive and sustainable trade policies

  • Share insights on how trade affects people, businesses, and the environment

  • Collaborate with a global network of youth leaders

  • Contribute to policy discussions and global trade conversations

Why This Opportunity Matters
Global trade remains a key driver of economic growth, job creation, and development. This programme provides young people with a platform to:

  • Influence global trade conversations

  • Build valuable international networks

  • Develop expertise in trade policy and global economics

It is particularly valuable for individuals interested in careers in international relations, economics, public policy, or development.

How to Apply
To apply:

  • Visit the official WTO application page

  • Prepare required materials (CV, short video, and written response)

  • Submit your application before the deadline
Digital Innovation Fund for Energy and Climate (DIFEC)

Applications are now open for the Digital Innovation Fund for Energy and Climate (DIFEC), a major funding opportunity supporting African enterprises building digital solutions for clean energy access and climate resilience. The programme is implemented by the

Africa Enterprise Challenge Fund (AECF) with support from the Swedish International Development Cooperation Agency (Sida).

DIFEC is designed to support innovative businesses leveraging technology to tackle pressing energy and climate challenges across Sub-Saharan Africa, while driving sustainable livelihoods and economic growth.

Why This Matters
Across Sub-Saharan Africa, millions of people still lack reliable access to clean energy, while climate change continues to threaten agriculture, livelihoods, and infrastructure. Digital innovation is playing a critical role in bridging these gaps—improving energy distribution, enabling climate adaptation, and powering smarter, data-driven solutions.

This fund goes beyond financing by providing the strategic support startups and growth-stage companies need to scale impactful solutions, transform communities, and build resilience.

Funding and Support
Selected enterprises will receive:

  • Non-repayable grants ranging from $150,000 to $400,000

  • Venture-building support to accelerate growth

  • Technical assistance to strengthen operations and improve investment readiness

Focus Areas
The fund supports digital and technology-driven solutions across key sectors, including:

  • Digital energy platforms and pay-as-you-go financing models

  • Climate data and advisory tools

  • E-mobility and clean transport systems

  • Clean cooking technologies

  • Climate-smart agriculture innovations

Eligibility Requirements

  • Startups at minimum viable product (MVP) stage and growth-stage companies

  • Must be operating within Sub-Saharan Africa

  • Demonstrate innovation, scalability, and measurable impact in energy access or climate resilience

Eligible Regions
Open to businesses operating across Sub-Saharan Africa.

How to Apply
Interested applicants can learn more and apply via the official link:

Applications are now open for the 2026 UNDP Pan-African Incubation Programme, an initiative designed to support high-potential AgriTech startups across Africa. Launched by the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) under the timbuktoo initiative, the programme aims to identify and scale innovative solutions driving Africa’s agricultural transformation.

The programme is delivered through the timbuktoo Pan-African Incubation Network and the timbuktoo AgriTech Hub, hosted in Ghana, with support from the Government of Japan.

About the Programme
 Selected startups will receive structured incubation support through accredited incubators within the timbuktoo ecosystem. In addition, participants will gain access to a continental network of founders, ecosystem partners, and investors.

Startups will also leverage the timbuktoo Pan-African digital platform, powered by Seedstars’ SIGMA platform, which provides:

  • Structured growth tools

  • Mentorship matching

  • Learning resources

  • AI-powered guidance for scaling and investment readiness

Eligibility Criteria
 Startups must:

The TWAS-CSIR Postgraduate Fellowship Programme is a fully funded opportunity for scholars from developing countries to pursue PhD research in science and technology fields in India.

  • Hosted at CSIR laboratories and institutes in India

  • Designed to strengthen scientific research capacity

  • Focuses on emerging areas of science and technology

Fellowship Types & Duration

  • Full-Time Fellowship

    • For candidates not yet enrolled in a PhD

    • Duration: Up to 4 years

  • Sandwich Fellowship

    • For candidates already enrolled in a PhD in their home country

    • Duration: 6 months to 3 years

Benefits

  • Monthly stipend covering:

    • Living expenses

    • Food

    • Health insurance

  • Access to subsidized accommodation

  • Note: Stipend is not convertible to foreign currency

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must:

  • Be nationals of a developing country (excluding India)

  • Be 35 years or younger by 31 December 2026

  • Hold a Master’s degree in science or technology

  • Be employed in their home country with a research role

  • Not hold residency/visa in India or any developed country

  • Provide proof of English proficiency (if applicable)

  • Show commitment to return to home country after completion

Additional Requirements:

  • Sandwich Fellowship:

    • Must be a registered PhD student

    • Provide a No Objection Certificate from home university

  • Full-Time Fellowship:

    • Must be willing to enroll in a PhD in India

Key Requirement

  • Applicants must secure an acceptance letter from a CSIR laboratory/institution before or by the application deadline

Application Process

  • Apply via the TWAS online application portal

  • Submit:

    • Academic documents

    • Research proposal

    • Acceptance letter from host institution

    • Required certificates (depending on fellowship type)

Important Notes

  • Fellowship is tenable only in India

  • Applicants can apply to only one TWAS/OWSD programme per year

  • Selected candidates typically begin their fellowship from 2027

ATKV x Global Changemakers Global Youth Summit

The ATKV x Global Changemakers Global Youth Summit 2026 in Cape Town, South Africa, is a fully funded leadership residency for high-impact young changemakers aged 21–28. The summit equips emerging leaders to scale community initiatives from local impact to global influence. Delegates also gain insights into Online MBA programs, graduate financial aid, and private student loans to accelerate professional growth. Whether your focus is fintech, healthcare innovation, or climate action, participation in this summit is a key step toward a career with global impact.

About the Summit

This flagship leadership program, organized in collaboration with ATKV, provides intensive workshops, cross-regional dialogues, and skill-building sessions designed to help participants scale their social impact initiatives. Beyond the residency, delegates benefit from ongoing mentorship and access to funding opportunities for community projects.

Who Should Apply

Eligible applicants must be emerging changemakers aged 21–28, with a strong working knowledge of English, currently leading a community initiative. Participants must be available for the intensive residency starting October 19, 2026, and should have a basic understanding of low-interest education loans or project grant management.

Key Benefits

Participants will receive:

  • Full coverage of return flights, accommodation, and meals

  • Access to mentorship, networks, and project training

  • Intensive workshops on leadership, innovation, and social impact

  • Eligibility to apply for post-summit community project funding

  • Exposure to graduate financial aid opportunities and global alumni networks

How to Apply

The application process involves five key phases:

1. Initiative Evaluation – Clearly define your project’s impact challenge, highlight its scalability, and demonstrate alignment with Global Changemakers’ mission.

2. Two-Minute Video – Record a professional 120-second video introducing yourself and your social impact work, highlighting your vision for a sustainable and inclusive world.

3. Written Application – Draft responses focusing on leadership, collaboration, and measurable outcomes. Discuss your prior support or fellowships if applicable.

4. Submission – Submit your video, written application, and application fee by April 20, 2026. Ensure high-risk fieldwork is covered under professional liability insurance if relevant.

5. Interview & Selection – Shortlisted applicants will complete a virtual interview assessing leadership and English proficiency. Successful candidates will finalize travel documents for Cape Town and prepare for post-summit mentorship and funding opportunities.

Tips for Applicants: Use the SMART method to set project goals (Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, Time-bound), apply the STAR technique (Situation, Task, Action, Result) in your video and written responses, and highlight collaboration and digital literacy skills.

The African Scholarship Programme offers early career researchers based in Africa the opportunity to spend approximately 90 days (3 months) at the Nordic Africa Institute in Sweden, working on and developing their ongoing Africa-focused research.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit their application through the official link, including:

  • Up-to-date CV with a list of publications

  • Work plan

  • Reference letter

This programme is a unique opportunity to advance Africa-focused research, gain international exposure, and contribute to global scholarly discourse.

The Niger Delta Development Commission (NDDC) is accepting applications for its 2026/2027 Foreign Post-Graduate Scholarship Programme. This initiative is designed to strengthen human resource development in the Niger Delta region by providing young professionals with advanced training and skills to participate effectively in Nigeria’s local content programmes and compete globally in their respective fields.

Eligible Fields of Study

Applicants must hold relevant Bachelor’s degrees in one of the following disciplines:

  • Agricultural Sciences / Biotechnology

  • Engineering

  • Environmental Sciences

  • Geosciences

  • Information Technology / Artificial Intelligence

  • Medical Sciences

  • Architecture / Building Technology

  • Law

Eligibility Criteria

  • Must be from the Niger Delta Region

  • Must have achieved a First Class or Second Class Upper undergraduate degree

  • Must be under 40 years of age

Selection Process

  1. Initial Application: Submit an online application via the NDDC website.

  2. Preliminary Shortlisting: Based on WASSCE/NECO results, degree classification, and local government identification.

  3. Computer-Based Test (CBT): Conducted by consultants in Rivers State; a minimum score of 70% is required to proceed.

  4. Further Shortlisting & Final Verification: Conducted through Local Government Areas (LGA) and Community Development Committees (CDC).

Note: Preference will be given to candidates from oil-producing host communities/local government areas who meet the cut-off marks.

Scholarship Details

  • Funding is for foreign postgraduate studies, with scholarship amounts calculated based on the prevailing exchange rate of the Naira.

  • Deferment, change of institution, or course is not permitted.

  • Selection is at the sole discretion of the NDDC, and the Commission’s decision is final.

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following documents online via www.nddc.gov.ng:

  • Recent passport photograph

  • Local Government Identification Letter

  • Postgraduate admission letter from a recognized overseas university

  • Relevant undergraduate degree certificates

  • NYSC Discharge / Exemption Certificate

After submission, applicants will receive a registration number; a printed copy of the acknowledgement is recommended for reference.

Contact Information

For enquiries, contact:

Director, Education, Health and Social Services, NDDC

  • Phone: +234(0)9137806550 | +234(0)9049409391

  • Email: foreign.scholarship@nddc.gov.ng

How to Apply

Submit applications online through the official NDDC Foreign Post-Graduate Scholarship portal.
One Million Black Women: Black in Business 2026

Applications are open for the One Million Black Women: Black in Business 2026 programme, a free business education initiative designed to help sole proprietors strengthen and scale their businesses.   5. Managing Your Financial Statements

Eligibility Criteria

The Caribbean BluEFin (Blue Economy Financing) Call for Proposals 2026 invites organisations to submit innovative project proposals that strengthen nature-based economic opportunities in the Caribbean while delivering measurable biodiversity, climate resilience, and socio-economic benefits. This initiative is part of the Caribbean Blue Economy Financing Project implemented by the Caribbean Biodiversity Fund (CBF) with funding from the Global Environment Facility (GEF) and support from the United Nations Environment Programme (UNEP).

Objective

The Call aims to identify, support, and showcase blue economy initiatives that:

  • Promote sustainable use of marine and coastal resources to generate livelihoods and growth

  • Deliver clear conservation and ecosystem resilience outcomes

  • Foster innovative and sustainable financing mechanisms

  • Mobilise additional public and private investment and demonstrate potential for scaling and replication

Funding and Benefits

Selected projects will receive grant financing between USD 200,000 and USD 250,000. Co-financing (cash and/or in-kind) is encouraged and considered an added value in the selection process.

Eligible Projects and Themes

Proposals should align with one or more of the following thematic areas:

  • Blue carbon and nature-based solutions, including sustainable fisheries and aquaculture value chains

  • Circular economy approaches to reduce marine pollution

  • Innovative blue finance mechanisms such as carbon or biodiversity credits and blended finance solutions

Eligibility Criteria

  • Projects must be implemented in one or more eligible BluEFin countries: Dominican Republic, Grenada, Saint Lucia, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, The Bahamas. Multi-country initiatives in these jurisdictions are permitted.

  • Applicants must be legally registered organisations (NGOs, civil society organisations, academic institutions, private sector entities, cooperatives, social enterprises, or consortiums with a lead organisation in an eligible country) with the necessary legal and financial documentation

  • Proposals must be submitted in English

  • Teams from all regions are encouraged to apply, including participants from Africa, Asia, Europe, the Americas, and Oceania, provided the project is implemented in one or more eligible Caribbean countries

How to Apply

Applicants should submit:

  • Completed project proposal using the CBF template

  • Detailed budget using the provided template

  • Articles of incorporation for the lead applicant (and partners where applicable)

  • Audited financial statements for the two most recent fiscal years

Proposals must be submitted electronically via the email address provided on the official Caribbean Biodiversity Fund website.

For full application guidelines and downloadable templates, visit the CBF official Call for Proposals page.

Location: Global (online submission)

About the Lens of Change Initiative

The Lens of Change programme is a rolling recruitment campaign designed to build a diverse network of young filmmakers aged 18 to 35. Selected participants will join a pre-vetted talent pool and be engaged on a project-by-project basis, enabling them to work on multiple assignments over time.

The initiative focuses on capturing real-world stories that highlight:

  • Youth-led advocacy

  • Community-driven impact

  • Social transformation across regions

By connecting filmmakers from Africa and Europe, the programme fosters cross-continental dialogue, collaboration, and the sharing of underrepresented stories with global audiences.

What Participants Will Do

Selected filmmakers will create compelling visual content to showcase the voices and experiences of young changemakers. Deliverables may include:

  • Short documentaries (3–15 minutes)

  • Social media video edits

  • Event highlight reels

These productions serve as tools to raise awareness, influence conversations, and promote social change within and beyond local communities.

Grants and Benefits

Participants selected for projects under the Lens of Change initiative will receive:

  • Project-based grants ranging from €765 to €1,800

  • Opportunities to collaborate with international partners

  • Exposure to global audiences and networks

  • Portfolio development through impactful storytelling projects

This combination of financial support and professional growth makes the programme a valuable opportunity for emerging creatives.

Who Should Apply

This call is ideal for young filmmakers who are passionate about storytelling and social impact. Applicants should have:

  • Experience in filming, storytelling, and video editing

  • A strong interest in social issues and community narratives

  • The ability to produce engaging and meaningful visual content

Creatives who are motivated to highlight youth voices and inspire change will find this opportunity particularly rewarding.

Countries & Regions Open to Application

The open call is available to young filmmakers from all countries in Africa and Europe, including but not limited to:

  • Africa: Nigeria, Kenya, South Africa, Ghana, Uganda, Ethiopia, Egypt, Morocco, and others

  • Europe: France, Germany, Italy, Spain, Netherlands, Sweden, Belgium, and more

Applicants from other regions are encouraged to review eligibility criteria to ensure their projects align with the programme’s goals.

Why This Opportunity Matters

The Lens of Change initiative goes beyond traditional filmmaking. It empowers young storytellers to:

  • Increase visibility of community impact

  • Strengthen connections between Africa and Europe

  • Highlight youth leadership in social change

Storytelling is a critical tool for influencing perspectives and driving action. This programme places young creatives at the heart of global social impact efforts.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should:

  • Review the Terms of Reference (TOR)

  • Submit applications via the official online forms, available in English and French

As this is a rolling recruitment process, early applications are encouraged to increase chances of selection for upcoming projects.

Final Thoughts

The Lens of Change open call offers more than a single project—it provides a pathway for long-term growth in global storytelling and social impact. Participants gain financial support, international exposure, and repeated opportunities to contribute to meaningful projects, positioning them as both creative professionals and social change advocates.

For young filmmakers ready to make an impact, this is a chance to tell stories that matter on a global stage.

Learn More & Apply Here

The Hollywood Foundation proudly presents the KwaZulu-Natal Bambelela Business Awards 2026, an initiative dedicated to celebrating and empowering local entrepreneurs across the province. These awards recognize the vital role of Small, Medium, and Micro Enterprises (SMMEs) in driving economic growth, creating jobs, and uplifting communities throughout KwaZulu-Natal. Beyond recognition, the programme offers meaningful support, visibility, and development opportunities to businesses aiming to grow and make a lasting impact.

Theme and Vision

The 2026 theme, “Bambelela (Hold On) – Your Dreams Are Valid,” celebrates the resilience and determination of entrepreneurs who continue to pursue their goals despite challenges. The awards aim to:

  • Encourage entrepreneurial resilience

  • Promote innovation and creativity

  • Support sustainable business growth

  • Strengthen community-driven enterprises

Through this initiative, the Hollywood Foundation reinforces its commitment to nurturing the next generation of business leaders in KwaZulu-Natal.

Programme Structure

The two-day programme is designed to showcase and develop participating businesses:

Exhibition Platform:
 Selected SMMEs will display their products and services to judges, stakeholders, and potential partners.

Seminar Sessions:
 Participants will engage in knowledge-sharing sessions to enhance business skills and strategic thinking.

Funding Pitch Opportunities:
 Entrepreneurs will present their business ideas or growth plans to a panel of judges, highlighting:

  • Innovation and uniqueness

  • Market potential

  • Community impact

This approach ensures participants gain both exposure and practical insights to strengthen their enterprises.

Awards and Funding

Winners will share R1 million in funding, provided by the Hollywood Foundation. This support is designed to:

  • Accelerate business growth

  • Enable expansion into new markets

  • Strengthen operational capacity

  • Ensure long-term sustainability

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Businesses must be 100% Black-owned

  • Based in KwaZulu-Natal with proof of registration and operation

  • Open to formal and informal businesses

  • Operational for at least 12 months

  • Submission of a business profile or detailed description of operations

Application Details

  • Applications Open: 2 March 2026

Applicants should carefully review all requirements and submit complete applications to maximize their chances of selection.

Programme Impact

The Bambelela Business Awards strengthen the entrepreneurial ecosystem by:

  • Creating jobs and promoting local economic development

  • Increasing visibility for emerging businesses

  • Enhancing access to funding and resources

  • Building capacity through training and mentorship

  • Promoting inclusive and equitable economic participation

By supporting SMMEs, the initiative contributes to resilient communities and sustainable regional development.

About the Hollywood Foundation

The Hollywood Foundation is dedicated to driving social impact through strategic initiatives in education, community upliftment, and enterprise development. Programs like the Bambelela Business Awards support individuals and businesses that have the potential to transform communities and contribute to broader economic progress.

Conclusion

The KwaZulu-Natal Bambelela Business Awards 2026 offer local entrepreneurs the chance to gain recognition, access funding, and elevate their businesses. By combining financial support with exposure and capacity-building opportunities, the programme empowers SMMEs to thrive in a competitive economic landscape.

Entrepreneurs across KwaZulu-Natal are encouraged to apply, showcase their innovation, and join a transformative journey toward sustainable success.

About the Program
The program identifies and rewards outstanding female researchers at the PhD and postdoctoral levels working in scientific fields such as:

  • Life and Environmental Sciences

  • Engineering and Technological Sciences

Funding & Benefits
Selected candidates will receive:

  •  €10,000 for PhD candidates

  •  €15,000 for postdoctoral researchers

  •  Recognition as a L’Oréal–UNESCO Young Talent

  •  Increased visibility in the global scientific community

Funds can be used for research advancement, equipment, conference participation, and professional development.

Eligibility Criteria
Applicants must:

  • Be female scientists from Sub-Saharan Africa

  • Be enrolled in a PhD program (at least 2nd year) or a postdoctoral program

Selection Criteria
Applications are evaluated on:

  • Scientific excellence and innovation

  • Academic achievements and publications

  • Impact and relevance of the research to society

Apply online

The ICANN87 Fellowship Program 2026 is now accepting applications. This initiative aims to strengthen multistakeholder participation by creating opportunities for individuals from underserved and underrepresented communities to become active participants in the ICANN community.

The Mila AI Policy Fellowship 2026 is a six-month paid program designed to bridge the gap between artificial intelligence research and public policy. FUNGAI DPI Governance in Africa Fellowship 2026

The FUNGAI DPI Governance in Africa Fellowship 2026 is a research and policy fellowship focused on the governance of Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) from a democratic and human rights perspective. The program is designed to bring together early‑career researchers, digital rights advocates, and policy thinkers from across Africa to investigate how digital systems—including identity frameworks, data platforms, and online service portals—shape state‑citizen relations.

Purpose and Focus:
 The fellowship seeks to deepen understanding of DPI governance through research and analysis that centers social justice, inclusion, accountability, and the impacts on marginalised populations. Fellows will examine how these digital systems contribute to inequality or promote rights and democratic participation, and will produce research outputs that inform policy, advocacy, and public discourse.

Program Structure:
 The curriculum is structured over several months and includes:

  • Foundational training on DPI, democratic governance, and research ethics.

  • Field research and documentation, including interviews and policy analysis.

  • Synthesis and knowledge translation into policy briefs, toolkits, and comparative outputs.

Stipend:
 Selected fellows receive a modest stipend totaling approximately USD 900 (about USD 300 per month) to support their participation in the program.

Eligibility:
 The fellowship is open to early‑career professionals and researchers based in African countries who are passionate about technology policy, digital governance, equity, and human rights. Relevant backgrounds include digital rights advocacy, policy research, social sciences, and related fields.

Application Process:
 Interested applicants must submit a complete application through the official FUNGAI Africa portal. Specific submission requirements are available via the programme’s website.
Globethics Emerging Leaders in Ethical AI Governance Fellowship 2026

The Globethics Emerging Leaders in Ethical AI Governance Fellowship 2026 is a competitive international fellowship designed for early‑career professionals working at the intersection of technology, ethics, policy, and governance. Hosted by Globethics—a global NGO dedicated to advancing ethical leadership and responsible governance—this programme equips selected fellows with the skills, networks, and practical experience needed to drive ethical, inclusive, and values‑driven approaches to artificial intelligence governance.

Who Can Apply:
 The fellowship targets early‑career professionals aged 24–35 from all regions, including but not limited to Africa, Asia, Europe, the Americas, and Oceania. Eligible applicants typically come from sectors such as academia (Master’s, PhD, postdoctoral researchers), government and public institutions, the private sector (AI, tech, innovation, compliance), civil society, or organisations engaged in intercultural and interfaith work. Applicants should demonstrate active engagement in AI ethics, governance, or responsible innovation, and the capacity to develop a project in these areas.

Fellowship Structure and Benefits:

  • A one‑week residency in Geneva, Switzerland (6–10 July 2026) at the heart of global policy dialogue and diplomacy.

  • Participation in the AI for Good Global Summit alongside global leaders shaping the future of governance and AI.

  • Mentorship from experts in AI governance and ethics.

  • Opportunity to develop and implement a research, policy, or applied project in ethical AI governance.

  • Core fellowship costs—including accommodation during the Geneva residency, programme fees, and summit access—are fully covered.

  • Top fellows may qualify for seed funding, continued mentorship, and visibility at the Global Ethics Forum in October 2026, including exposure to high‑level partners and donors.

Application Requirements:
 Applicants must submit a single PDF containing:

  • A CV (maximum two pages).

  • An expression of interest (approximately 500 words) outlining:

    • Background in AI, governance, ethics, or interfaith engagement.

    • Motivation for joining the fellowship.

    • An initial idea for a research, policy, or innovation project.

  • Optional supporting materials, such as publications, portfolio, or a letter of recommendation.

This fellowship offers a rare opportunity for emerging thought leaders across the world to deepen their expertise, expand their professional networks, and contribute to shaping ethical and inclusive governance frameworks for AI.

The programme aims to build the capacity of future health leaders and equip them with research, policy analysis, and governance skills to influence how digital technologies transform healthcare worldwide.

For the 2026 cohort, eight research fellows will be selected to work on topics such as:

  • National policies for young people’s digital well‑being

  • Mapping digital health services for young people

  • Digital citizenship for health

  • AI competencies and the health workforce

  • Emerging roles in an AI‑enabled health workforce


Who Should Apply

This fellowship is open to:

  • University students

  • Early‑career researchers

  • Young professionals with interest or experience in digital health, public policy, health systems, technology, or governance

Eligible applicants are primarily based in low‑ and middle‑income countries (LMICs), including regions in Africa, Asia, Latin America, the Caribbean, the Middle East, and other eligible LMIC areas.


What You Will Gain

Selected fellows will:

  • Gain hands‑on research experience in digital health policy and governance

  • Collaborate with global health experts and researchers

  • Enhance skills in research methodology, policy analysis, and evidence‑based advocacy

  • Contribute to research that informs global debates on digital health transformation

  • Develop professional networks across sectors and regions


Why This Opportunity Matters

As digital technologies reshape healthcare systems worldwide — from AI integration to health data ecosystems — there is a growing need for capable leaders who understand both the technical and policy aspects of these transformations. How to Apply

  • Have a minimum of 7 years of public sector experience.

  • Be aged 35–54 years.


Benefits

Participants will:

  • Receive world-class executive leadership training.

  • Gain skills and frameworks to improve public sector effectiveness, integrity, and organisational culture.

  • Build a professional network across African public sector leaders.

  • Apply learning through a capstone project addressing real institutional challenges.


How to Apply

To apply:

  1. Visit the official AIG Public Leaders Programme page:

  2. Complete the application form and submit required documents.

Applications are now open for the 2026 Code for Africa (CfA) Fellowships, offered through CfA’s African Fact-Checking Alliance (AFCA). This stipend-based fellowship equips journalists and fact-checkers in Southern Africa with skills and tools to investigate, debunk misinformation and disinformation, and strengthen information integrity across the region.

The fellowship is part of the project “Strengthening Information Integrity and Democratic Resilience in Southern Africa”, supported by Norway’s Ministry of Foreign Affairs. It builds on the 2024–2025 phase and expands efforts to improve democratic resilience through high-quality fact-checking.


Focus Countries

The fellowship is open to candidates based in the following Southern African countries:

Angola, Botswana, Comoros, Democratic Republic of Congo, Eswatini, Lesotho, Madagascar, Malawi, Mauritius, Mozambique, Namibia, Seychelles, South Africa, United Republic of Tanzania, Zambia, and Zimbabwe.


Eligibility Criteria

Candidates must:

  • Be established journalists or digital storytellers with at least one year of media experience and a portfolio of published work.

  • Preferably have experience in fact-checking.

  • Be affiliated with a media partner, with work published or broadcast through mainstream media.

  • Be non-partisan and not employed by paramilitary or security organisations.

  • Be fluent in English and/or French, depending on the official language of their country.

  • Have reliable access to a computer and stable internet.

  • Be willing to publish work on public platforms; CfA may assist with partnerships or syndication.

  • Commit to sharing new skills with peers and their organisation.

  • Be available for virtual meetings and online mentoring.


Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows will receive:

  • Six-month stipend-based fellowship supporting investigative and fact-checking work.

  • Editorial training and mentorship from CfA’s PesaCheck, iLAB, and AFCA teams.

  • Hands-on technical training and one-on-one mentorship, including guidance from technologists, data analysts, multimedia producers, and editors.

  • Publishing support to disseminate their projects locally and internationally.

  • Opportunity to enhance or launch flagship CheckDesks in their newsrooms.

The fellowship allows participants to produce impactful fact-checking articles highlighting misinformation in Southern Africa while strengthening their professional network and skills.


How to Apply

To apply:

  1. Visit the official Code for Africa Fellowship webpage.

  2. Complete the online application form and submit all required documents.

  3. Ensure your application is submitted before the deadline
